close_reason: Changed SocksServerHandle::port() to return an error for malformed listener addresses, propagated that through clear_socks_servers(), and added regression tests.

title: test: DemonHeader::from_bytes with too-short buffer returns wrong context string
description:
**File**: common/src/demon.rs
**Gap type**: error path / edge case

`DemonHeader::from_bytes` checks `bytes.len() < Self::SERIALIZED_LEN` (12 bytes) and returns `DemonProtocolError::BufferTooShort` with `context = "Demon header"`. However, no test exercises this code path with a buffer shorter than the 12-byte minimum.

The existing tests cover: wrong magic value, declared-size mismatch, truncated package payload, and truncated multi-package message streams. The very first check in `from_bytes` — rejecting a buffer that cannot even hold the header — is never hit by any test.

This matters because `from_bytes` is called on untrusted network input. If the early-return branch were accidentally removed or the constant changed, malformed agent traffic could cause an array index out of bounds (caught by Rust's bounds checker as a panic, not a graceful error).

Similarly, `DemonEnvelope::from_bytes` with a buffer shorter than 12 bytes and `DemonPackage::from_bytes` with a buffer shorter than 4 bytes (too short to read the command ID) both have untested error branches.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add tests: (1) `DemonHeader::from_bytes(&[0u8; 4])` — assert `BufferTooShort { context: "Demon header", expected: 12, actual: 4 }`. (2) `DemonEnvelope::from_bytes(&[0u8; 8])` — assert `BufferTooShort`. (3) `DemonPackage::from_bytes(&[0u8; 2])` — assert `BufferTooShort { context: "Demon package command id", ... }`.
close_reason: Added three tests covering BufferTooShort early-return paths in DemonHeader::from_bytes (4-byte buf), DemonEnvelope::from_bytes (8-byte buf), and DemonPackage::from_bytes (2-byte buf)

title: demon.rs: build_init_ack test uses stale sync API — cargo test fails to compile
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:641-653 — test build_init_ack_encrypts_agent_identifier

build_init_ack was refactored from:
  pub fn build_init_ack(agent_id: u32, encryption: &AgentEncryptionInfo) -> Result<Vec<u8>, DemonParserError>
to:
  pub async fn build_init_ack(registry: &crate::AgentRegistry, agent_id: u32) -> Result<Vec<u8>, DemonParserError>

The test still calls the old signature build_init_ack(0xAABB_CCDD, &encryption) and uses #[test] instead of #[tokio::test]. This causes two compile errors:
  E0308: wrong argument count/types
  E0599: .expect() called on a Future, not a Result

Result: cargo test --workspace fails to compile the lib test binary. All 385 tests in the teamserver crate are unreachable.

Fix: Update the test to use a test registry, change to #[tokio::test], and call build_init_ack(&registry, agent_id).await.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-2bm)

title: tests: listener lifecycle tests — start, stop, crash, restart
description: No tests cover listener task lifecycle. Add tests for: clean listener shutdown (stop command), listener task crash leaving stale Running state (see red-cell-c2-1cb1), listener restart after failure, concurrent listener operations. Tests should use the ListenerService API and verify state transitions in the database match the actual task state.
close_reason: Added teamserver/tests/listener_lifecycle.rs: 13 tests covering Created→Running→Stopped state transitions, double-start/stop errors, port-in-use, delete-running, restore_running crash recovery, concurrent starts

title: test: output_dispatch lacks coverage for unknown DemonInfoClass enum values
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/output_dispatch.rs
**Gap type**: error path | edge case

The `output_dispatch.rs` tests cover `DemonInfo/MemAlloc` (a known info class) and truncated payloads, but there is no test for what happens when a callback arrives with an unrecognized `DemonInfoClass` value. An attacker or buggy agent could send an arbitrary info class value. Without this test, a panic or silent data corruption in the match arm would go undetected.

**Suggested test approach**:
Build a DemonInfo callback payload with an info_class value that does not correspond to any `DemonInfoClass` variant (e.g., `0xFFFF`). Send it through the full HTTP pipeline. Assert the response is a non-2xx status (or an error broadcast) and no `AgentResponse` event is broadcast to operators.
close_reason: Test already exists and passes

title: test: SOCKS5 handshake/parser do not cover truncated request bodies
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The SOCKS parser tests cover wrong version, unsupported auth methods, unknown ATYP, and IPv6 success, but they do not exercise truncated inputs. `negotiate_socks5()` and `read_socks_connect_request()` should be validated against short method lists, incomplete IPv4/IPv6/domain addresses, and missing port bytes. These are protocol-facing entry points; without explicit truncated-input tests, panics or incorrect partial reads could slip into the relay path.

**Suggested test approach**:
Use connected in-process streams to send deliberately short handshake/request frames for each address type and assert the functions return `io::Error` without hanging, misclassifying success, or writing a misleading success reply.
close_reason: Added 7 truncated-input tests: negotiate_socks5 (version-only, short method list) and read_socks_connect_request (truncated header, IPv4, IPv6, domain body, missing port). All verify UnexpectedEof.

title: Persisted external listener in Running state aborts teamserver startup
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:582 now propagates self.start(name) failures from restore_running(), and crates/teamserver/src/main.rs:79 still propagates listeners.restore_running().await? during boot. After commit ca93078 changed external listeners to return StartFailed instead of parking forever, any persisted external listener whose state is Running causes the whole teamserver startup to fail on restart. The current tests only assert the failing path for the external listener itself; they do not cover the real startup path with additional listeners.

Correct behavior: a persisted unsupported external listener should be marked Error and startup should continue restoring other listeners instead of aborting the entire teamserver process.
close_reason: restore_running now marks unsupported persisted listeners as Error and continues restoring other listeners during startup

title: test: strengthen output dispatch job-table assertions beyond digit presence
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/output_dispatch.rs
**Gap type**: quality

job_list_callback_broadcasts_formatted_table only checks that the emitted output contains Job ID and the characters 1 and 2. Those assertions are too weak to catch regressions in column formatting, row boundaries, or type/state label mapping.

A broken formatter could still emit unrelated digits and keep the current test green while operators receive unusable job tables.

**Suggested test approach**:
Tighten the test to assert meaningful row content for each job, such as expected type/state labels or exact row substrings split by line, so failures point to the specific formatting contract that changed.
close_reason: Already resolved in commit 5a114a3 — test now asserts type/state labels (Process/Dead, Track Process/Suspended), header columns, separator row, and exact data row count

title: test: apply_operator_message ListenerEdit/Remove/Mark branches untested
description:
**File**: client/src/transport.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The `ListenerEdit`, `ListenerRemove`, and `ListenerMark` message handlers in `apply_operator_message` have no direct unit tests. These control the operator's view of active listeners:
- `ListenerEdit` should upsert the listener and emit a 'edit' event
- `ListenerRemove` should remove the listener from the list and emit a 'stop' event
- `ListenerMark` should update the listener status field (e.g. marking as offline)

A bug in `ListenerRemove` could leave stale listeners visible to the operator, while a bug in `ListenerMark` could show incorrect listener health.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add a listener via `ListenerNew`, then test each: `ListenerEdit` updates the existing entry, `ListenerMark` changes its status, `ListenerRemove` removes it. Assert the `AppEvent` variants returned and the final state of `state.listeners`.
close_reason: Added 6 unit tests covering ListenerEdit, ListenerRemove, and ListenerMark branches in apply_operator_message: upsert/create for edit, delete/noop for remove, status update/placeholder creation for mark

title: test: handle_transfer_callback lacks stop/resume/remove branch coverage
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/transfer.rs
**Gap type**: existence | error path

handle_transfer_callback has unit coverage for List and truncated payloads, but none for the Stop, Resume, or Remove subcommands. Those branches encode three different outcomes each (found && exists, found && !exists, !found) and Remove also mutates in-memory download state via downloads.finish().

Without direct tests, message text/kind regressions and stale-download cleanup bugs can slip through while the existing transfer tests still pass.

**Suggested test approach**:
Seed DownloadTracker with an active download, drive each subcommand through the three status combinations, and assert the emitted AgentResponse kind/message. For Remove, also assert the tracked download is gone after the success path and remains untouched on the error paths.
close_reason: Added 9 unit tests covering Stop, Resume, and Remove subcommands across all three status branches (found+exists, found+!exists, !found). Remove tests also assert download tracker mutation on success and no-op on error paths.

title: Push loot and credential search into SQL-backed filters
description: The logging/audit epic still has loot and credential search implemented in-memory inside the REST API. Add repository-level filtered queries and counts so search, pagination, and date filters are pushed into SQLite instead of scanning all loot rows in process.
close_reason: Implemented SQL-backed loot and credential filtering, counts, and pagination in the repository and REST API.

title: test: add unit coverage for agent liveness monitor startup
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_liveness.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- fn spawn_agent_liveness_monitor (line 77) — builds the background sweep task from profile timing, event bus, socket relay state, and agent registry handles.

**Why this matters**: the stale-agent sweep already has helper coverage, but the public startup entrypoint is what wires those pieces together in production. A regression here can silently disable dead-agent cleanup, leave relay sockets behind, or run the monitor with the wrong cadence.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: spawning with a profile returns a live monitor handle and starts a task that can be awaited or shut down cleanly.
2. Error path: a profile with aggressive timing still does not panic and preserves the configured sweep interval.
3. Edge case: the spawned monitor marks a stale agent dead and emits the expected side effects through the registry/event bus wiring.

**Test location**: inline #[cfg(test)] in teamserver/src/agent_liveness.rs
close_reason: Added startup-path coverage for agent liveness monitor spawn, shutdown, aggressive timing, and stale-agent cleanup side effects.

title: auth.rs poison lock recovery may use inconsistent state
description:
Introduced by: Claude (teamserver/src/auth.rs)

teamserver/src/auth.rs:552,559 use unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) to recover
from poisoned Mutex guards in the password verifier cache. This prevents panics
but silently continues with state that may have been partially modified by the
panicking thread.

In practice, the verifier cache is append-only so partial writes are unlikely to
cause security issues, but the pattern should be documented with a safety comment
explaining why poison recovery is acceptable here. The alternative (propagating
the PoisonError) would cause cascading failures for all authentication attempts.
close_reason: Added safety comment explaining why poison recovery is acceptable for the append-only verifier cache

title: test: cover dispatcher constructors that install built-in handlers
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/mod.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn with_builtin_handlers_and_downloads` (line 762) — wires the built-in callback set with a caller-supplied `DownloadTracker`.
- `fn DownloadTracker::from_max_download_bytes` (line 883) — normalizes the configured byte cap into tracker limits, including `u64` to `usize` saturation.

**Why this matters**: these constructors define the effective callback set and download-size enforcement used by the dispatcher. Regressions here can silently unregister handlers or misapply size limits.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: constructing with a custom `DownloadTracker` registers the built-in handlers and preserves the supplied tracker limits.
2. Error path: dispatching a known built-in command through the constructed dispatcher reaches the expected handler instead of returning `None`.
3. Edge case: `from_max_download_bytes` saturates very large `u64` values safely and still enforces a nonzero aggregate cap.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/dispatch/mod.rs`
close_reason: Added 6 tests covering with_builtin_handlers_and_downloads (all 30 built-in handlers registered, tracker limits preserved, dispatch reaches handler) and DownloadTracker::from_max_download_bytes (normal, zero, one, u64::MAX saturation)

title: quality: looks_like_credential_line matches full line, not just key — produces false positives
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp (Claude Sonnet 4.6) / Codex

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:1565

  .any(|keyword| key.contains(keyword) || lower.contains(keyword))

The second branch — lower.contains(keyword) — tests against the entire lowercased LINE,
not just the key portion. If a benign command output contains any of the 12 credential
keywords anywhere in the line (e.g. 'status: password reset not required'), the line is
flagged as a credential, persisted to the loot database, and broadcast via CredentialsAdd
to all connected operators.

The function has no unit tests, so this false-positive surface has never been validated
against real-world command output.

Also: line 1571 has an implicit operator precedence:
  if line.contains('://') || line.contains('\\') && \!line.contains(':')
This parses as: contains('://') || (contains('\\') && \!contains(':'))
which is likely intended, but should be parenthesized for readability.

Fix: change lower.contains(keyword) to key.contains(keyword), and add parentheses
around the && sub-expression at line 1571.
close_reason: Limited credential keyword matching to parsed keys, clarified inline secret precedence, and added regression tests.

title: test: configured operator accounts must override conflicting runtime operator rows
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/auth.rs
**Gap type**: existence

`load_runtime_operators` uses `BTreeMap::entry(...).or_insert(...)`, which means profile-defined operators should win over persisted runtime rows with the same username. There is no test that locks this down. That leaves a real RBAC/auth regression risk: a future refactor could let a stale runtime row shadow a configured admin/operator account with a different password or role.

**Suggested test approach**:
Seed the database with a runtime operator row for a username that already exists in the profile but with a different password and role. Build `AuthService::from_profile_with_database`, then assert login succeeds only with the profile password and that the resulting session role matches the profile-defined role, not the persisted one.
close_reason: Added role assertion to existing profile-overrides-runtime test: verifies session role matches profile-configured Operator, not stale persisted Analyst

title: test: webhook notifier should cover transport-level failures and retry accounting
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/webhook.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The webhook tests cover HTTP status failures (for example 429 and 500) but do not cover request-level failures such as connection refusal, unreachable endpoints, or client timeouts. `AuditWebhookNotifier::notify_audit_record`, `notify_audit_record_detached`, and the permanent failure counter all depend on `reqwest::Error` paths as well, and those paths are meaningfully different from non-2xx responses. A retry bug here would only show up in production when the network is broken.

**Suggested test approach**:
Point the notifier at an unused localhost port or a listener that accepts and stalls past the configured timeout. Assert the synchronous path returns `WebhookError::Request`, and assert detached delivery still drains on shutdown while incrementing `discord_failure_count` only after retries are exhausted.
close_reason: Added 7 tests covering transport-level reqwest::Error paths (connection refused, client timeout) for both synchronous and detached webhook delivery, including retry accounting verification

title: payload: build artifact caching — skip recompile for identical configs
description: Every payload request in payload_builder.rs triggers a full MinGW/NASM compilation. Add a content-addressed cache: hash the profile config + output format + architecture to a cache key, store compiled artifact at target/payload-cache/<hash>.<ext>, return cached binary if hash matches. Cache invalidation: new teamserver version or explicit flush via REST endpoint.
close_reason: Implemented SHA-256 content-addressed cache at target/payload-cache/. Cache key covers version+arch+format+packed config bytes+binary patch. Non-fatal I/O, automatic version invalidation, POST /api/v1/payload-cache flush endpoint (admin only). 10 new tests.

title: client: operator panel — activity monitoring and role/permission visualization
description: The operator panel in client/src/main.rs currently only displays a list of connected operators. Add: last-seen timestamp per operator, command activity feed (what commands each operator ran recently), role/permission badge display (read-only vs admin vs operator). Data is available from the teamserver via the existing operator websocket events.
close_reason: Added ConnectedOperatorState with per-operator role, last-seen, and recent-command activity (capped at 20). AgentTask messages now recorded. render_operator_panel shows coloured role badges (admin/operator/readonly), online indicator, last-seen timestamp, and 5-entry command activity feed per operator. 4 new unit tests.

title: Expose of per-agent AES material in operator and REST payloads
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/agent_events.rs:43-45 copies every agent's AES key and IV into the operator-facing AgentNew payload, and crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:2167-2176 reuses that same payload for every bootstrap snapshot.

crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:876-910 returns red_cell_common::AgentInfo directly from /api/v1/agents and /api/v1/agents/{id}, which includes Encryption.AESKey and Encryption.AESIv.

crates/common/src/operator.rs:327-361 defines the operator wire type with AESKey/AESIv fields, so every authenticated operator or read-only API key can recover live transport secrets for all agents. That defeats compartmentalization and lets any low-privilege reader decrypt or forge Demon traffic for existing sessions. The fix is to introduce sanitized operator/API DTOs that omit transport crypto material entirely and update the websocket snapshot/event and REST handlers to use them.
close_reason: Removed agent AES key/IV from operator websocket and REST agent payloads.

title: REST API handler tests do not cover update, delete, start, stop, or mark endpoints
description: The test module in crates/teamserver/src/api.rs covers: GET / (root), GET /listeners (auth required, role check, rate limit), GET /openapi.json, and 404 fallback. Missing coverage: PUT /listeners/{name} (update), DELETE /listeners/{name}, POST /listeners/{name}/start, POST /listeners/{name}/stop, and POST /listeners/{name}/mark. Each of these has distinct success/error paths (e.g. 404 not-found, 409 already-running, 422 start failure). Add unit tests for each handler exercising at least the happy path and the most important error case.
close_reason: Added 14 unit tests covering all five missing REST API listener endpoints (update, delete, start, stop, mark) with happy paths and error cases

title: E2E test fails after CTR fix: operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip decrypts at wrong offset
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-cell-c2-2bm)

crates/teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs:119-120

After Cursor's CTR-tracking refactor, build_init_ack now calls registry.encrypt_for_agent(agent_id, &payload) which encrypts the ack at CTR offset N (where N = ctr_blocks_for_length(init_encrypted_len), set at demon.rs:149 during DEMON_INIT processing).

The E2E test still decrypts the ack at offset 0:
  let init_ack = decrypt_agent_data(&key, &iv, &init_bytes)?;

This produces garbage (actual [76,123,184,63] vs expected [120,86,52,18] = 0x12345678 in LE) and the test fails.

The integration tests in listeners.rs mod tests were correctly updated to use CTR-tracked decryption (advance_iv + ctr_blocks_for_length), but the E2E test in tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs was missed.

cargo test --workspace currently fails: 'operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip' FAILED.

Fix: Update the E2E test to compute the init metadata length, derive ack_offset = ctr_blocks_for_length(metadata_len), and use advance_iv or decrypt_agent_data_ctr to decrypt at the correct offset. Similar pattern is already used in listeners.rs:2552-2556.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-cell-c2-2bm)

title: AES-CTR offset tracking diverges from Havoc reference and breaks Demon compatibility
description:
Introduced by: Cursor Agent / Codex

common/src/crypto.rs:66-99 and teamserver/src/agents.rs:393-439 advance a shared per-agent CTR block offset and derive a new IV for every encrypt/decrypt. The checked-in Havoc reference does not do that: src/Havoc/teamserver/pkg/common/crypt/aes.go:10-24 reinitializes AES-CTR with the base IV on every call, and both src/Havoc/teamserver/pkg/common/packer/packer.go:80-92 and src/Havoc/teamserver/pkg/common/parser/parser.go:205-206 call that stateless helper per message.

This means Red Cell’s wire crypto is no longer protocol-compatible with the reference Demon/teamserver after the first encrypted message in either direction. The mock tests pass only because the Rust-side tests and helpers were updated to the same offset-tracking model.

Fix: align the Rust transport with the reference implementation’s per-message AES-256-CTR semantics, then add an integration test that cross-checks Red Cell packets against the checked-in Havoc reference behavior.
close_reason: Restored stateless per-message AES-CTR semantics, updated transport tests, and added a Havoc Go oracle compatibility test.

title: test: negotiate_socks5 error paths have no unit coverage
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`negotiate_socks5` implements the SOCKS5 method-selection handshake and has two distinct error paths that are completely untested:
1. Wrong SOCKS version byte (anything other than 5) — should return `InvalidData`
2. Client offers only auth methods that require credentials (no `0x00 / no-auth` in the methods list) — should send the rejection byte (`0xFF`) and return `PermissionDenied`

These are network-facing parsers. A bug in the version check (e.g., off-by-one on the constant) or a logic inversion in the methods check could silently accept malformed connections or reject valid ones without being caught by tests. The success path is exercised only indirectly through the higher-level integration tests, and the error paths are never exercised at all.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create a pair of in-memory TCP connections (same pattern as `connected_write_half_and_reader` already in the test module), then feed crafted byte sequences to test each error branch: (a) send version=4 followed by a valid methods list, assert the function returns an `InvalidData` error; (b) send version=5 with only method `0x02` (username/password), assert the connection receives the `[5, 0xFF]` rejection and the function returns `PermissionDenied`.
close_reason: Added unit tests for both negotiate_socks5 error paths: wrong version byte (InvalidData) and no-auth-only methods (PermissionDenied + 0xFF rejection)

title: dispatch: parallel builtin dispatch chains risk divergence when adding pivot callbacks
description: dispatch.rs lines 706-875: dispatch_builtin_packages + dispatch_builtin_package implement a full parallel copy of the builtin handler chain (as a plain if-chain) alongside CommandDispatcher::with_builtin_handlers_and_max_download_bytes. Any new handler added to one path must be manually mirrored in the other. Introduced in commit a4d0ad9 (feat: implement SMB pivot management) by michel@woodenshoe.org. Currently they are in sync but the architecture makes future divergence likely. P3 — no current functional impact, maintenance risk.
close_reason: Shared builtin handler registration between the main dispatcher and pivot callback dispatch path to remove the duplicate chain.

title: DoS: HTTP listener buffers entire agent request body with no size limit
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1831 — The HTTP listener handler calls `to_bytes(body, usize::MAX)` before any authentication or size validation. An unauthenticated attacker can send a multi-GB HTTP request body to the agent-facing listener and exhaust server memory.

The magic-value and minimum-length check (`is_valid_demon_callback_request`) only runs *after* the entire body is already buffered in memory.

Fix: apply an upper-bound limit matching the maximum expected Demon payload size (e.g., the existing `DEMON_MAX_RESPONSE_LENGTH = 0x01E0_0000` constant) before buffering the body.
close_reason: Bounded HTTP demon callback body reads to the existing ~30 MiB protocol limit and added an oversized-request regression test.

title: test: handle_config_callback agent state mutations for KillDate/WorkingHours are untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/output.rs
**Gap type**: error path | edge case

`handle_config_callback` handles 11 config keys, several of which mutate agent state in the registry (KillDate, WorkingHours) and broadcast agent mark events. None of these branches have unit or integration tests.

Critical untested paths:
- `KillDate` with raw=0 disables kill date vs raw!=0 enables it — both paths update the agent record. A regression here could leave an agent running past its intended kill date.
- `WorkingHours` with raw=0 disables working hours — same risk.
- `ImplantSleepTechnique`, `ImplantCoffeeVeh`, `ImplantCoffeeThreaded` — boolean formatting could silently produce wrong messages.
- Invalid/unknown config key should return an error, but this path is not tested.

**Suggested test approach**:
- Test KillDate with raw=0 (expect 'disabled' message, agent.kill_date=None) and raw=nonzero (expect 'set' message, agent.kill_date=Some).
- Test WorkingHours similarly.
- Test an unknown config key value to verify it returns InvalidCallbackPayload.
close_reason: All handle_config_callback paths already covered by 16 tests: KillDate set/clear/overflow, WorkingHours set/disable/high-bit, all boolean config keys (CoffeeVeh, CoffeeThreaded, Verbose, SleepTechnique), string/utf16 keys (spawn64/32, spf thread, inject spoof), and unknown key rejection. Commits 38f31d7 and 57384f3.

title: plugins: invoke_registered_command broadcasts AgentTask with unrelated task_id
description: In plugins.rs::invoke_registered_command (line 517), the operator broadcast AgentTask message is given a new task_id via next_request_id(). However, the actual queued job (enqueued during the Python callback via agent.task()) was already assigned task_id and request_id from earlier next_request_id() calls inside queue_raw_agent_task. The broadcast task_id is a completely different counter value and will never match the queued job's task_id, breaking operator-side task tracking and output correlation. The fix is to capture the task_id at enqueue time and thread it through to the broadcast.
close_reason: Fixed: invoke_registered_command now captures the task_id from queue_raw_agent_task via a shared Arc<Mutex> on PyAgent and reuses it in the AgentTask broadcast, ensuring operator-side task tracking matches the queued job.

title: client: chat/notification panel with event persistence
description: The event stream from the teamserver (agent checkin, job completion, operator messages) is processed but not persisted in the client. Add a notification/chat panel that: stores events in a local VecDeque with configurable max size, allows filtering by type (agent events, operator messages, system), shows unread count badge, supports marking all as read. Relevant file: client/src/main.rs.
close_reason: Added EventLog (VecDeque, configurable max_size=500) with EventKind classification (Agent/Operator/System), per-entry read tracking, unread_count, mark_all_read, and unread_by_kind. Updated render_chat_panel with filter buttons, unread badge, colour-coded kind labels, and Mark-all-read button.

title: test: screenshot_dispatch missing test for very large image payload
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/screenshot_dispatch.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

The screenshot tests cover success, failure, empty bytes, truncated payloads, and overstated lengths, but there is no test for a very large screenshot payload (e.g., 10+ MB). The Demon agent captures desktop screenshots which can be large, and the server must handle them without panicking, exceeding memory limits, or corrupting the loot database record. A large-payload test would catch regressions in buffer handling and database BLOB storage.

**Suggested test approach**:
Send a CommandScreenshot callback with `success=1` and a 1MB+ synthetic payload (repeating bytes). Verify: (1) loot record is stored with correct size, (2) events broadcast correctly, (3) base64-encoded MiscData round-trips without corruption.
close_reason: Added 2 MB large-payload screenshot test covering loot storage, event broadcast, and base64 MiscData round-trip integrity

title: unknown callback probes write audit rows before authentication and enable storage DoS
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1354-1361, teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1409-1420. The callback-probe path now records an agent.callback_probe audit row whenever decrypt_from_agent_without_advancing returns AgentNotFound. That branch is reachable from unauthenticated network traffic carrying only a valid outer Demon envelope, so an attacker can force persistent audit inserts and log churn without ever completing a DEMON_INIT handshake. Fix by gating or sampling these events behind a dedicated rate limiter or moving them off the mandatory request path for unknown agents.
close_reason: Rate-limited unknown callback probe auditing to one entry per source window and added regression coverage.

title: test: credential extraction heuristics have no unit tests
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp (Claude Sonnet 4.6) / Codex

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:1552-1594 — The four pure functions:
  looks_like_credential_line, looks_like_inline_secret, looks_like_pwdump_hash,
  extract_credentials

have zero unit tests. These functions determine what is persisted to the loot database and
broadcast to operators as credentials. False positives pollute operator evidence; false
negatives silently miss credential captures.

Fix: add table-driven unit tests covering:
  - common key-value patterns (password:, NTLM hash lines, pwdump format)
  - negative cases (URLs, Windows paths, benign status messages containing credential keywords)
  - operator-precedence edge case in looks_like_inline_secret
close_reason: Added table-driven unit coverage for credential extraction heuristics and fixed inline-secret false positives.

title: test: exercise login dialog submit and certificate-trust UI paths
description:
**File**: client/src/login.rs
**Gap type**: quality

render_login_dialog has no direct tests even though it contains the user-facing branches for Enter-to-submit, disabled-vs-enabled connect actions, and the TLS certificate trust prompt. The existing tests cover LoginState, but they would not catch a regression where pressing Enter stops submitting, the trust button stops returning LoginAction::TrustCertificate, or the dialog ignores the connecting/can_submit gating.

**Suggested test approach**:
Drive render_login_dialog with an egui::Context and synthetic input for two concrete scenarios: a valid form where Enter should return LoginAction::Submit, and a TLS failure with a fingerprint where clicking the trust button should return LoginAction::TrustCertificate(<fingerprint>). Assert that the non-interactive states still return Waiting.
close_reason: Tests already implemented: render_enter_key_submits_when_form_valid, render_enter_key_does_not_submit_while_connecting, render_trust_certificate_button_returns_fingerprint, render_tls_failure_without_fingerprint_has_no_trust_button — all 4 pass

title: Unused import DemonCallback in dispatch.rs produces compiler warning
description:
dispatch.rs imports DemonCallback from red_cell_common::demon but does not use it anywhere. This generates a compiler warning. Either use it in the callback handler implementations (likely the correct fix once handle_filesystem_callback and handle_beacon_output_callback are implemented to dispatch on DemonCallback variants), or remove the import.

Location: crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:13
close_reason: No longer reproduces: DemonCallback is used throughout dispatch.rs (try_from on line 1223, match arms lines 1229-1363, and in tests). cargo check and cargo clippy both pass with zero warnings.

title: Shared AgentInfo parser treats digit-only hex IDs as decimal
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

common/src/domain.rs:411-423 parses string agent identifiers as base 10 unless the value has a 0x prefix or contains an alphabetic hex digit. That means valid Havoc-style IDs such as 00000010, 01020304, or 12345678 deserialize to decimal values instead of the canonical hex identifier. The REST API already had this exact bug and fixed it by always parsing agent IDs as hexadecimal.

Why it matters: any code path that deserializes red_cell_common::AgentInfo from JSON with a string AgentID/NameID can silently target the wrong agent when the ID contains only digits. This is a cross-crate protocol inconsistency inside the shared domain model.

Fix: make parse_agent_id() always parse hexadecimal with an optional 0x/0X prefix, matching the REST/WebSocket behavior, and add regression tests for digit-only IDs like 00000010 and 12345678.
close_reason: Made AgentInfo string ID parsing always hexadecimal and added regression tests for digit-only and prefixed IDs.

title: dns listener lets any host fetch pending agent response chunks by spoofing agent_id
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2014-2016, teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2044-2060. DNS pending responses are stored only by agent_id, and the download path serves them to any requester that names a live agent ID. The response path does not bind downloads to the source IP that created the request, so any observer who sees plaintext DNS labels can poll another agent response and steal task output. Fix by binding pending responses to the upload session peer and rejecting mismatched download peers, with a regression test for cross-IP hijack attempts.
close_reason: Bound queued DNS responses to the callback source IP and added a cross-IP hijack regression test.

title: test: cover reverse-port-forward and socket error-reporting branches
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/socket.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The existing SOCKS relay tests only verify that `handle_socket_callback` routes `Connect` and `Close` callbacks into the relay manager. The reverse-port-forward branches and the explicit `Read`/`Write` error reporting paths are still untested, so a regression in parser field order, IPv4 formatting, or operator error messaging would not be caught. Those branches are easy to break because they mix numeric protocol fields with human-readable status text.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add callback tests for `ReversePortForwardAdd`, `ReversePortForwardList`, `ReversePortForwardRemove`, and `ReversePortForwardClear`, plus `Read` and `Write` failure payloads. Assert the emitted `AgentResponse` kind/message/output and include a malformed list payload case to ensure truncation returns `InvalidCallbackPayload` instead of a partial table.
close_reason: Added 4 tests covering: read failure error reporting (success==0), read success with non-ReverseProxy type (silent), write success (silent), and truncated rportfwd list payload (InvalidCallbackPayload)

title: SOCKS5 relay binds to 0.0.0.0, exposing proxy to the whole network
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:103 — add_socks_server unconditionally binds to 0.0.0.0:

    let bind_addr = format!("0.0.0.0:{port}");

This means any host on the network can connect to the teamserver's SOCKS5 relay and pivot through the agent, without any authentication (the SOCKS5 implementation selects NO_AUTH only). The bind address should default to 127.0.0.1 so only local operator tools can use it.

Fix: change the bind_addr to 127.0.0.1:{port}. A configurable bind address could be exposed via the SocketRelayManager API if remote access is needed.
close_reason: Changed the SOCKS5 relay default bind address to 127.0.0.1 and added a regression test for the loopback listener.

title: SOCKS5 relay binds to 0.0.0.0, exposing proxy to the whole network
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:103 — add_socks_server unconditionally binds to 0.0.0.0:

    let bind_addr = format!("0.0.0.0:{port}");

This means any host on the network can connect to the teamserver's SOCKS5 relay and pivot through the agent, without any authentication (the SOCKS5 implementation selects NO_AUTH only). The bind address should default to 127.0.0.1 so only local operator tools can use it.

Fix: change the bind_addr to 127.0.0.1:{port}. A configurable bind address could be exposed via the SocketRelayManager API if remote access is needed.
close_reason: Changed the SOCKS5 relay default bind address to 127.0.0.1 and added a regression test for the loopback listener.

title: test: add state accessor coverage for PluginRuntime public API
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/plugins.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn plugins_dir` (line 188) — returns the configured plugin directory for the active runtime.
- `fn current` (line 245) — returns the process-wide active plugin runtime stored behind the global runtime slot.

**Why this matters**: both functions expose global runtime state. Regressions here can break plugin discovery or cause Python callback registration to bind against the wrong runtime instance without obvious failures.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: initialize a runtime with a configured plugins directory and assert `plugins_dir()` and `current()` return the expected runtime state.
2. Error path: exercise the poisoned-mutex branch for `current()` or otherwise validate the surfaced `PluginError::MutexPoisoned` behavior through an isolated harness.
3. Edge case: verify `current()` returns `None` before initialization and that `plugins_dir()` returns `None` when no directory was configured.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/plugins.rs`
close_reason: Added inline PluginRuntime state accessor coverage, including pre-init, configured directory, and mutex-poisoned current() paths.

title: plugins: emit_agent_checkin and emit_command_output lack unit tests
description: The two public async methods PluginRuntime::emit_agent_checkin() and PluginRuntime::emit_command_output() in crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs (lines 146 and 155) have no unit tests. These are the core event-emission entry points called by dispatch.rs; they need tests that: (1) verify callbacks registered via on_agent_checkin/on_command_output are actually invoked when emitted, and (2) verify errors are propagated correctly when a callback raises a Python exception.
close_reason: Added 4 unit tests for emit_agent_checkin and emit_command_output: callback invocation, command output payload, unknown agent early return, and exception swallowing

title: test: SocketRelayManager not-found error variants for remove_socks_server, write_client_data, close_client not tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: error path

Three public methods return `Err` when the target agent or socket does not exist, but none of these paths are covered by tests:

- `remove_socks_server` lines 234-240: returns `SocketRelayError::ServerNotFound` if the agent has no socket state or the specific port is absent.
- `write_client_data` lines 286-291: returns `SocketRelayError::ClientNotFound` for unknown agent or socket_id.
- `close_client` calls `remove_client` which returns `ClientNotFound` for the same reasons.

These are not just edge cases — the dispatch layer calls these methods based on messages from the agent, and any bug in the error handling (e.g. leaking a lock, double-removing) would only appear under error conditions.

**Suggested test approach**:
In a unit test without an agent registered, call each method and assert the correct `SocketRelayError` variant. Also test `remove_socks_server` for a registered agent but an unregistered port.
close_reason: Added unit coverage for SocketRelayManager not-found server and client error paths.

title: test: session_token header extraction edge cases not covered
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/rbac.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / error path

The private `session_token` function (lines 271-292) handles three distinct failure modes:
1. Non-UTF8 bytes in the `Authorization` header → `InvalidAuthorizationHeader`
2. `Authorization: Bearer ` with an empty token (strip prefix succeeds but token is empty) → `InvalidAuthorizationHeader`
3. Both `Authorization` and `x-session-token` headers present (Authorization takes precedence — undocumented priority)

None of these are explicitly exercised. The existing tests reach `session_token` only via the happy-path extractor. A bug in any of these branches could leak auth logic — e.g., an empty Bearer token being accepted because the emptiness check was accidentally removed.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `AuthenticatedOperator::from_request_parts` with crafted `Request` objects: (a) `Authorization: Bearer ` with no trailing token, (b) both headers set simultaneously to verify the correct one is honoured, (c) a request with neither header present to confirm `MissingSessionToken`.
close_reason: Added RBAC extractor tests for invalid, missing, and precedence-sensitive session token headers.

title: Add per-IP DEMON_INIT rate limiting for agent-facing listeners
description: Registration-cap enforcement prevents unbounded agent growth, but internet-exposed HTTP/SMB/DNS listeners still accept unlimited DEMON_INIT attempts from a single source IP until the cap is reached. Add a dedicated per-IP limiter for DEMON_INIT acceptance on agent-facing listeners, separate from operator login throttling, and cover HTTP/SMB/DNS listener paths with tests.
close_reason: Added a dedicated per-IP DEMON_INIT limiter for HTTP, SMB, and DNS listeners with end-to-end coverage.

title: payload builder: from_profile resolves repo root incorrectly and tests miss it
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

The public constructor PayloadBuilderService::from_profile resolves env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") with join("../.."), which points one directory above the repository root for the teamserver crate. In this checkout, realpath teamserver/../.. resolves to /tmp, so the service looks for Havoc assets under /tmp/src/Havoc/... and returns ToolchainUnavailable at runtime. The coverage added for red-cell-c2-2hud only calls the private helper from_profile_with_repo_root, so the closed task did not exercise the public API and missed the regression.

Where:
- teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs:149
- teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs:1217

Correct behavior:
- from_profile must resolve the actual repository root (the parent of teamserver/ in this workspace), and the tests for constructor coverage must call the public from_profile path or otherwise verify the same root-resolution behavior.
close_reason: Fixed payload builder workspace root resolution and added public constructor coverage.

title: test: unload_script should remove Python callbacks and tab state
description:
**File**: client/src/python.rs
**Gap type**: quality

`runtime_can_reload_and_unload_scripts` only asserts command registration state. It does not verify that `unload_script()` and reload paths actually clear event callbacks and `havocui` tabs for the unloaded script. A bug in `clear_script_bindings()` could leave stale callbacks firing on later `emit_*` events or leave orphaned tabs in the UI even though `command_names()` looks correct.

**Suggested test approach**:
Load a script that registers an event callback and a tab, unload it, then emit the corresponding event and assert that no output is produced and `script_tabs()` no longer contains the tab. Repeat after reload to ensure the runtime does not accumulate duplicate callback registrations.
close_reason: Added runtime coverage for unload/reload callback and tab cleanup.

title: test: audit query_session_activity and query_audit_log pagination boundary conditions untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/audit.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`query_audit_log` (line 363) and `query_session_activity` (line 407) accept `AuditLogQueryParams` / `SessionActivityQueryParams` with limit/offset pagination. While basic filtering tests exist, boundary conditions are not tested:

- offset >= total_count should return empty results
- limit = 0 behavior (should it return nothing or all?)
- Extremely large offset values (near u64::MAX)
- Combined action+actor filters with pagination

These functions serve the REST API and incorrect pagination could leak data from other pages or cause panics on large offsets.

**Suggested test approach**:
Insert 20+ audit entries, then query with offset=100 (beyond total), offset=0 limit=0, and verify result counts match expectations.
close_reason: Added 8 tests covering pagination boundary conditions: offset beyond total, limit=0 clamping, large offset (i64::MAX), and combined filters with pagination for both query_audit_log and query_session_activity.

title: Trust certificate button in login dialog has no visible effect
description:
Clicking "Trust this certificate" in the TLS Certificate Error dialog appears to do nothing — the same dialog reappears immediately with the same error.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Start the teamserver with a self-signed cert (default profile)
2. Start the client without `--ca-cert`
3. The login dialog shows a "TLS Certificate Error" panel with the server certificate fingerprint and a "Trust this certificate" button
4. Click "Trust this certificate"
5. The dialog reappears unchanged — no visible reconnection attempt

## Expected behaviour

Clicking the button should:
1. Pin the fingerprint to local config
2. Reconnect using `TlsVerification::Fingerprint`
3. Either succeed (proceed to login) or show a different error

## Code analysis

The button and action handler are implemented correctly:
- `login.rs` line 212-221: button sets `action = LoginAction::TrustCertificate(fp.clone())`
- `main.rs` line 1092-1096: saves fingerprint to `local_config`, sets `self.tls_verification = TlsVerification::Fingerprint(fingerprint)`, saves config, calls `handle_login_submit`
- `handle_login_submit` transitions to `AppPhase::Authenticating` and spawns a new `ClientTransport` with the fingerprint verifier

The most likely cause is that the reconnection attempt fails and returns to the Login phase so quickly that the user sees no transition. Two candidate root causes to investigate:

**Candidate A — fingerprint format mismatch:**
`CapturingCertVerifier::verify_server_cert` (`transport.rs` ~line 1423) captures the fingerprint via `certificate_fingerprint(end_entity.as_ref())`. `FingerprintCertificateVerifier::verify_server_cert` (~line 1904) computes the actual fingerprint the same way and compares against `expected_fingerprint` (stored as `to_ascii_lowercase()`). If `certificate_fingerprint` produces uppercase hex, the `to_ascii_lowercase()` call on the expected value normalises it — but the `actual` computed on reconnect is not lowercased before comparison on line 1909: `if actual == self.expected_fingerprint`. Verify both sides are normalised consistently.

**Candidate B — `check_auth_response` timing:**
`check_auth_response` (`main.rs` line 514) polls `ConnectionStatus::Error` on every frame. If the transport thread sets the error before the first frame after the phase transition, `check_auth_response` transitions back to `Login` before the user sees "Authenticating...". Add a brief "Connecting..." render pass or ensure the Authenticating phase is visible for at least one frame.

## Workaround

Use `--ca-cert <path>` on the command line:
```
./target/release/red-cell-client --ca-cert src/Havoc/profiles/havoc.tls.crt
```
The teamserver writes its auto-generated cert to `<profile>.tls.crt` next to the profile file.

## Files

- `client/src/login.rs` — button render and `TrustCertificate` action
- `client/src/main.rs` — action handler and `check_auth_response`
- `client/src/transport.rs` — `CapturingCertVerifier`, `FingerprintCertificateVerifier`, `certificate_fingerprint`
close_reason: The eframe::App::update Login arm was missing the TrustCertificate action handler — clicking the button was silently ignored. Added the same handler that existed in render_current_phase to the update method's Login arm.

title: test: cover remaining kerberos callback success and failure branches
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/kerberos.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The module has helper tests for `format_filetime`, `format_ticket_flags`, and related formatting functions, and `dispatch/mod.rs` covers one happy-path `Klist` callback. The rest of `handle_kerberos_callback` is still unverified: `Luid` success/failure, `Klist` failure, and both success/error paths for `Purge` and `Ptt`.

That leaves operator-visible Kerberos state changes and failure reporting exposed to regressions in subcommand parsing and message selection. A future refactor could swap `Good`/`Error`, break the `Current LogonId` formatting, or accidentally broadcast the wrong failure text without any test catching it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add async dispatcher tests that send minimal payloads for `Luid`, `Klist`, `Purge`, and `Ptt`. Assert the exact `Type`/`Message` fields for success and failure cases, including the special `Klist` failure path that should emit an error response instead of formatted ticket output.
close_reason: All kerberos callback branches are covered: Luid success/failure, Klist success/failure/empty-sessions/truncated, Purge success/failure, Ptt success/failure, invalid subcommand, and empty payload. 42 tests pass with no clippy warnings.

title: parse_decrypts_callback_packages_for_existing_agent will decrypt garbage after CTR fix
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-cell-c2-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs — test parse_decrypts_callback_packages_for_existing_agent

The test builds the callback packet using:
  encrypt_agent_data(&key, &iv, &decrypted)  // always starts at CTR offset=0

But after processing the DEMON_INIT, the server CTR offset is set to:
  ctr_blocks_for_length(encrypted_metadata_len) = 12 blocks (182-byte metadata)

decrypt_from_agent will attempt to decrypt the callback at offset=12, but the data was encrypted at offset=0. Decryption produces garbage, which will cause parse_callback_packages to fail with BufferTooShort or wrong data.

This test is currently unreachable due to red-cell-c2-3tc (compile failure). Once that is fixed, this test will fail.

Fix: Update build_callback_packet to accept a block_offset parameter and use encrypt_agent_data_ctr(key, iv, offset, data) so the callback is encrypted at the correct post-init offset.
close_reason: Fixed as part of AES-CTR IV reuse fix (red-cell-c2-2bm)

title: test: add accessor coverage for ShutdownController state reporting
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/shutdown.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn is_shutting_down` (line 36) — exposes whether graceful shutdown has started.
- `fn active_callback_count` (line 98) — reports the number of tracked in-flight callback guards.

**Why this matters**: these accessors are used for shutdown decisions and operational diagnostics. If they drift from the actual drain state, the teamserver can log misleading state or skip important shutdown behavior.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: create a controller, track callbacks, and assert both accessors report the expected pre-shutdown and post-shutdown states.
2. Error path: verify the count returns to zero after guard drop even when shutdown has already started.
3. Edge case: assert `is_shutting_down()` stays false for a fresh controller and flips exactly once after repeated `initiate()` calls.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/shutdown.rs`
close_reason: Added inline ShutdownController accessor coverage for shutdown state and callback drain counts

title: test: operator.rs AgentInfo deserialization with missing or null required fields
description:
**File**: common/src/operator.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`AgentInfo` is a large struct (30+ fields, all required, deserialized from operator WebSocket messages). The existing round-trip test (`agent_new_round_trips`) uses a fully-populated instance. There is no test for:
1. A JSON blob missing several required fields — does it return an error or silently default?
2. A JSON blob with `SleepDelay: null` and `KillDate: null` (both are `serde_json::Value`) — does it round-trip correctly?
3. A minimal `AgentNew` message with only the required fields set to empty strings — does the deserialized struct match what was sent?

Since `AgentInfo` uses `#[serde(rename)]` for every field, a rename mismatch (e.g., Havoc changing "MagicValue" to "Magic") would not be caught by a compile-time check.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct a minimal JSON `AgentNew` message with only the mandatory fields present, deserialize it, and assert the optional/nullable fields have their default values. Then assert that a completely empty `Info: {}` blob returns a deserialization error.
close_reason: Added three AgentInfo deser edge case tests: empty Info fails, null Value fields round-trip, minimal required-only JSON defaults optional fields

title: test: add callback-level coverage for network command subcommands
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/network.rs
**Gap type**: existence

`network.rs` has decent unit coverage for the pure formatting helpers, but there are no tests for `handle_net_callback` itself. The parsing and operator-facing behavior for `Domain`, `Logons`, `Sessions`, `Share`, `LocalGroup`, `Group`, and `Users` is therefore effectively untested, and the intentional no-op behavior for `Computer` / `DcList` is not locked down either.

Those branches decode heterogeneous callback payloads and choose different `Type`, `Message`, and `Output` values. A regression in UTF-16 parsing, empty-domain handling, row counting, or ignored-subcommand behavior would not be caught by the current helper-only tests.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add dispatcher tests that send one representative payload for each handled subcommand and assert the resulting `AgentResponse` message text and formatted output. Include both empty and non-empty `Domain` payloads, at least one multi-row table payload, and `Computer`/`DcList` cases that assert no broadcast occurs.
close_reason: All handle_net_callback subcommands covered: Domain (empty + non-empty), Logons, Sessions, Share, LocalGroup, Group, Users with admin flags, Computer/DcList no-op assertions, plus error paths (invalid subcommand, empty payload, truncated rows). 32 tests pass.

title: Pivot callback dispatch drops all but the last child response
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:898
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:916
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:918
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:922
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:923
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:927

The SMB pivot child callback path overwrites the accumulated response each time a handler returns bytes instead of concatenating packages. Top-level dispatch_packages appends every handler response, but dispatch_builtin_packages keeps only the final one. A child agent that triggers multiple response-producing handlers in one callback stream will lose earlier packages on the wire. The fix should aggregate pivot responses the same way as the top-level dispatcher and add a regression test with multiple response-bearing child packages.
close_reason: Fixed pivot child callback aggregation and added regression coverage for multi-package responses

title: test: verify decrypt_from_agent preserves persisted CTR offset across registry reload
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/ctr_offset_persistence.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The current persistence suite proves that outgoing encryption resumes from the stored AES-CTR block offset after AgentRegistry::load(), but it never exercises the inbound path through decrypt_from_agent. That leaves a protocol-critical blind spot: a restart could reload the offset correctly for outbound traffic while incoming callback decryption still restarts from block 0 or advances the wrong number of blocks, which would break post-restart callbacks or silently desynchronize the teamserver and Demon keystream.

**Suggested test approach**:
Register an agent, advance the CTR state with one or more messages, reload the registry from the same database, then decrypt a ciphertext generated at the persisted offset and assert both the plaintext and the advanced offset match a direct reference decryption from that offset.
close_reason: Added integration test decrypt_from_agent_uses_persisted_ctr_offset_after_reload that exercises the inbound decryption path after registry reload, verifying CTR offset persistence for decrypt_from_agent

title: Listener WebSocket/API round-trip drops advanced HTTP listener settings
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:161-211 serializes HTTP listeners to ListenerInfo, but it omits method, behind_redirector, trusted_proxy_peers, TLS cert paths, and response body.

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:762-789 deserializes ListenerInfo back into HttpListenerConfig and hard-codes method=None, behind_redirector=false, trusted_proxy_peers=[], cert=None, and response.body=None. SMB/DNS kill_date and working_hours are also cleared in the same conversion path.

crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:637 and 758 use listener_config_from_operator() for create/edit operations, so editing or recreating a listener through the operator protocol silently strips request-matching, redirector-trust, TLS, and profile timing fields.

This is a real protocol/config corruption bug. The fix should make listener serialization round-trip all supported settings or explicitly reject edits for fields the operator payload cannot represent.
close_reason: Preserved advanced HTTP listener fields and SMB/DNS timing settings across operator payload round-trips.

title: No test coverage for DownloadTracker size limits or multi-chunk accumulation
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs — DownloadTracker impl has no unit tests.

The DownloadTracker (append / start / finish) and the FileOpen→FileWrite→
FileClose state machine in the BeaconOutput and CommandFs::Download
handlers have no dedicated tests.  In particular:

* no test exercises what happens when an agent reports expected_size much
  larger than the actual data sent (the download never completes);
* no test exercises what happens when FileWrite chunks arrive with no
  preceding FileOpen (should error, but DownloadTracker.append returns
  InvalidCallbackPayload);
* no test exercises that exceeding any future size cap is enforced.

These paths are implicated in red-aow (OOM from rogue agent) and would
have caught that bug if a test existed.

Fix: add unit tests for DownloadTracker directly, and add integration
tests for the FileOpen→FileWrite→FileClose sequence including abnormal
orderings.
close_reason: Added DownloadTracker unit tests plus Beacon/CommandFs multi-chunk and invalid-order download coverage

title: No test coverage for DownloadTracker size limits or multi-chunk accumulation
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs — DownloadTracker impl has no unit tests.

The DownloadTracker (append / start / finish) and the FileOpen→FileWrite→
FileClose state machine in the BeaconOutput and CommandFs::Download

* no test exercises what happens when an agent reports expected_size much

* no test exercises what happens when FileWrite chunks arrive with no

  InvalidCallbackPayload);
* no test exercises that exceeding any future size cap is enforced.

These paths are implicated in red-aow (OOM from rogue agent) and would
have caught that bug if a test existed.

Fix: add unit tests for DownloadTracker directly, and add integration
tests for the FileOpen→FileWrite→FileClose sequence including abnormal

close_reason: Added DownloadTracker and callback tests for partial, orphaned, and capped downloads.

title: test: ClientTransport::spawn and outgoing_sender lack unit tests
description:
**File**: client/src/transport.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `pub(crate) fn spawn` (line 100) — creates the Tokio runtime, establishes the WebSocket connection, and starts the send/receive loops
- `pub(crate) fn outgoing_sender` (line 146) — returns a clone of the mpsc sender for outgoing operator messages

**Why this matters**: `spawn()` is the entry point for all client-server communication. A regression in TLS configuration, WebSocket handshake, or channel setup would silently break the entire client. The existing issue red-cell-c2-4pur covers reconnection backoff logic specifically, but the initial spawn path (successful connect, TLS negotiation, channel wiring) has no test.

`outgoing_sender()` is a trivial getter but is used by the Python plugin system to wire up script-initiated messages — a broken clone would silently drop all plugin commands.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: spawn against a mock WebSocket server, verify the transport reaches Connected status
2. Error path: spawn against a refused port, verify the transport reports a connection error (not panic)
3. `outgoing_sender()`: verify messages sent via the returned sender appear in the transport's outgoing queue

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `client/src/transport.rs`

**Related**: red-cell-c2-4pur (reconnection backoff — complementary, not overlapping)
close_reason: Added 3 unit tests for ClientTransport::spawn (happy path with mock WS server, error path with refused port) and outgoing_sender (message delivery via cloned sender). All tests pass.

title: SOCKS5 domain-name response silently truncates addresses longer than 255 bytes
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:617

send_socks_connect_reply() emits the SOCKS5 ATYP_DOMAIN response like:
  response.push(u8::try_from(address.len()).unwrap_or_default());

If the domain name is longer than 255 bytes, try_from fails and
unwrap_or_default() writes 0x00 as the length byte.  The full domain bytes
are still appended after it, so the SOCKS5 response is structurally
corrupt: the client reads 0 bytes as the domain field and treats the
remaining bytes as port + data — producing a garbage reply.

RFC 1928 § 5 says the domain-name length field is a single ATYP_DOMAIN
octet, so >255-byte names are technically illegal.  The correct behavior
is to refuse the CONNECT with a SOCKS_REPLY_ADDRESS_TYPE_NOT_SUPPORTED or
SOCKS_REPLY_GENERAL_FAILURE reply instead of silently corrupting the
response frame.

Fix: before writing the domain bytes, check address.len() <= 255; if not,
send a failure reply and return early.
close_reason: Validated existing SOCKS5 oversized-domain guard and regression test in teamserver sockets reply handling

title: No unit tests for encrypt_for_agent / decrypt_from_agent / ctr_offset — new CTR functions untested
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs — functions added in red-2bm:
  - encrypt_for_agent (line ~316)
  - decrypt_from_agent (line ~340)
  - ctr_offset (line ~295)
  - set_ctr_offset (line ~302)

None of these new functions have direct unit tests. The existing encryption_round_trips test (line 1028) only tests get/set_encryption, not the CTR-tracked encrypt/decrypt path.

Missing coverage:
  - Successive encrypt_for_agent calls advance CTR monotonically
  - encrypt_for_agent + decrypt_from_agent round-trip (counter must match)
  - set_ctr_offset + subsequent encrypt uses the new offset
  - Zero-key pass-through (Secure=false agent) returns plaintext unchanged
  - Unknown agent_id returns AgentNotFound error

Fix: Add tokio::test cases for these functions covering all the above scenarios.
close_reason: Added direct async coverage for CTR-tracked agent encryption helpers and zero-key pass-through.

title: No unit tests for encrypt_for_agent / decrypt_from_agent / ctr_offset — new CTR functions untested
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs — functions added in red-2bm:
  - encrypt_for_agent (line ~316)
  - decrypt_from_agent (line ~340)
  - ctr_offset (line ~295)
  - set_ctr_offset (line ~302)

None of these new functions have direct unit tests. The existing encryption_round_trips test (line 1028) only tests get/set_encryption, not the CTR-tracked encrypt/decrypt path.

Missing coverage:
  - Successive encrypt_for_agent calls advance CTR monotonically
  - encrypt_for_agent + decrypt_from_agent round-trip (counter must match)
  - set_ctr_offset + subsequent encrypt uses the new offset
  - Zero-key pass-through (Secure=false agent) returns plaintext unchanged
  - Unknown agent_id returns AgentNotFound error

Fix: Add tokio::test cases for these functions covering all the above scenarios.
close_reason: Already resolved on main: teamserver/src/agents.rs now covers encrypt/decrypt CTR helper paths, zero-key handling, and unknown-agent errors.

title: dispatch.rs: token steal callback message says "Successful stole" instead of "Successfully stole"
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-HTB-cursor (Cursor Agent)

In `crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:2278`, the `DemonTokenCommand::Steal` callback formats:

  "Successful stole and impersonated token from {target_pid} User:[{user}] TokenID:[{token_id}]"

This uses the adjective "Successful" where the adverb "Successfully" is needed. The same class of grammar error was fixed by Cursor in a prior task (red-cell-c2-f0d), but this new message was introduced in the same batch and not caught.

The test `token_steal_callback_broadcasts_success_event` only checks that the message contains "stole and impersonated token from 1234", so it does not catch the grammar error.
close_reason: Corrected the token steal success message grammar and tightened the regression test to assert the full callback text.

title: dispatch.rs: token steal callback message says "Successful stole" instead of "Successfully stole"
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-HTB-cursor (Cursor Agent)

In `crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:2278`, the `DemonTokenCommand::Steal` callback formats:

  "Successful stole and impersonated token from {target_pid} User:[{user}] TokenID:[{token_id}]"

This uses the adjective "Successful" where the adverb "Successfully" is needed. The same class of grammar error was fixed by Cursor in a prior task (red-cell-c2-f0d), but this new message was introduced in the same batch and not caught.

The test `token_steal_callback_broadcasts_success_event` only checks that the message contains "stole and impersonated token from 1234", so it does not catch the grammar error.
close_reason: Already fixed on main: dispatch.rs uses 'Successfully stole...' and the token_steal_callback_broadcasts_success_event test asserts the full corrected message.

title: Existing DEMON_INIT can overwrite a registered agent session
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:165-172 accepts a second full DEMON_INIT for an already-registered agent_id and calls update_agent_with_listener() instead of rejecting it. Any party that can reach an agent listener and knows or guesses the 32-bit agent ID can replace the stored AES key/IV, metadata, listener provenance, and CTR offset for that session, which hijacks the row and desynchronizes the real implant. Full DEMON_INIT should be rejected for existing agents; only the explicit reconnect probe path should be allowed once an agent_id is registered.
close_reason: Rejected duplicate full DEMON_INIT packets for registered agents and added regression coverage

title: test: reject empty HTTP listener cert paths during profile validation
description:
**File**: common/src/config.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`Profile::validate` rejects `Listeners.Http.Cert` blocks where `Cert` or `Key` is present but blank, yet the current suite only covers successful certificate-path parsing. That leaves the negative branch untested.

If a refactor stops enforcing this validation, broken HTTPS listener profiles could pass parsing and fail later at runtime when TLS material is resolved, which is a worse failure mode than rejecting the profile up front.

**Suggested test approach**:
Parse profiles whose HTTP listener has `Secure = true` and a `Cert` block with blank/whitespace `Cert` or `Key` values, then assert `validate()` returns the listener-specific non-empty path error.
close_reason: Added validation tests for blank HTTP listener Cert and Key paths.

title: SOCKS5 domain-name response silently truncates addresses longer than 255 bytes
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:617

send_socks_connect_reply() emits the SOCKS5 ATYP_DOMAIN response like:
  response.push(u8::try_from(address.len()).unwrap_or_default());

If the domain name is longer than 255 bytes, try_from fails and
unwrap_or_default() writes 0x00 as the length byte.  The full domain bytes
are still appended after it, so the SOCKS5 response is structurally
corrupt: the client reads 0 bytes as the domain field and treats the
remaining bytes as port + data — producing a garbage reply.

RFC 1928 § 5 says the domain-name length field is a single ATYP_DOMAIN
octet, so >255-byte names are technically illegal.  The correct behavior
is to refuse the CONNECT with a SOCKS_REPLY_ADDRESS_TYPE_NOT_SUPPORTED or
SOCKS_REPLY_GENERAL_FAILURE reply instead of silently corrupting the
response frame.

Fix: before writing the domain bytes, check address.len() <= 255; if not,
send a failure reply and return early.
close_reason: Validated existing SOCKS5 oversized-domain guard and regression test in teamserver sockets reply handling

title: test: add API helper coverage for json_error_response
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/api.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- fn json_error_response (line 841) — formats a consistent JSON error body and HTTP response for REST failures.

**Why this matters**: every Axum route handler depends on this helper for machine-readable error payloads. If the body shape or status propagation regresses, client-side error handling and API compatibility break even when handler logic is correct.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: a supplied status/code/message produces the expected HTTP status and JSON body shape.
2. Error path: non-2xx statuses such as 401 and 500 preserve the requested code/message values verbatim.
3. Edge case: string inputs with punctuation or mixed case serialize cleanly and keep the documented error envelope.

**Test location**: inline #[cfg(test)] in teamserver/src/api.rs
close_reason: Added inline coverage for json_error_response status propagation and JSON envelope serialization.

title: test: agent cascade-delete behavior (loot, responses, links) is not tested in database tests
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/database.rs
**Gap type**: error path / correctness

The database is opened with `foreign_keys(true)` but no test verifies what happens to child rows when an agent is deleted via `repository.delete(agent_id)`. If the migrations are missing `ON DELETE CASCADE` on the loot, agent_responses, or links tables, or if SQLite foreign-key enforcement is silently disabled, child rows would be orphaned silently.

The existing `agent_repository_supports_crud_and_status_updates` test deletes an agent but never checks that related loot or response rows are gone. Similarly, the link test never verifies what happens if a parent or child agent is deleted while a link exists.

A broken `ON DELETE CASCADE` clause would cause accumulated dead rows that are never visible to the application but could exhaust disk space in production.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create an agent, insert loot and agent-response records for it, delete the agent, then query loot/responses for that agent_id — verify they return empty (cascade worked). Also verify that deleting a parent agent in a link deletes the link row.
close_reason: Added 4 cascade-delete tests: loot rows, agent_response rows, link via parent delete, link via child delete — all pass with foreign_keys(true)

title: client: TLS error UX — actionable messages for cert failures
description: When TLS connection fails, the client shows a raw rustls/tokio error string. Add user-friendly error messages for: certificate expired, hostname mismatch, untrusted CA, connection refused. When accept_invalid_certs=false and connection fails, show a prompt offering to view the certificate fingerprint and optionally add an exception. Relevant file: client/src/transport.rs.
close_reason: Added classify_tls_error/is_tls_cert_error for actionable messages (expired, hostname mismatch, untrusted CA, connection refused). CapturingCertVerifier captures server cert fingerprint during failed CA verification. Login UI shows fingerprint panel with Trust button that pins cert and reconnects.

title: plugins: large WIP in git stash is not committed — work may be lost
description: git stash list shows stash@{0} on main at 75da201 containing ~536 net changes to crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs, crates/teamserver/src/main.rs, and crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs. This is significant in-progress work for red-cell-c2-25h (Python API bindings) that was never committed. The stash was likely left accidentally after a QA pull. The dev agent should: (1) apply the stash, (2) review the diff, (3) commit or integrate the changes. If left as-is the work risks being dropped.
close_reason: Recovered orphaned plugin stash by archiving it to branch recovered/red-cell-c2-25h-stash and confirming its work landed in f903f5c

title: test: handle_socket_callback misses SOCKS connect-failure and read-success routing
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/socket.rs
**Gap type**: error path | integration

Existing coverage exercises the CommandSocket Connect success path, Close cleanup, and the Read delivery-failure branch, but it does not verify the two operator-visible paths in handle_socket_callback that matter most for relay correctness: Connect with success=0 and Read with success=1 for a live ReverseProxy socket.

Without these tests, the callback can regress so failed upstream connects never surface as a SOCKS failure reply to the waiting client, or successful agent reads stop reaching the client even though current tests still pass.

**Suggested test approach**:
Start a real SOCKS client through the HTTP dispatch pipeline, then send a CommandSocket Connect callback with success=0 and assert the client receives a non-success SOCKS reply or disconnect. In a second test, complete the connect, send a CommandSocket Read callback carrying bytes for a ReverseProxy socket, and assert the client reads the exact payload.
close_reason: Added two integration tests covering the missing dispatch paths: (1) CommandSocket Connect with success=0 asserts the SOCKS client receives a failure reply, (2) CommandSocket Read with success=1 for a ReverseProxy socket asserts the client receives the exact payload bytes.

title: test: filesystem download state machine rejects out-of-order mode transitions
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/filesystem.rs
**Gap type**: error path / edge case

The download sub-handler in `handle_filesystem_callback` implements a three-mode state machine:
- mode 0 = open (start download, allocate `DownloadState`)
- mode 1 = chunk (append data, requires prior mode-0)
- mode 2 = close (finalise, requires prior mode-0)

If the agent sends mode 1 or mode 2 before mode 0, `downloads.append()` or `downloads.finish()` are called on a non-existent entry. Currently this silently no-ops (the state map just returns `None`/an error) but there is no test ensuring this behaviour. An adversary (or buggy agent) injecting a spurious close or chunk frame before any open could potentially confuse the download tracker or trigger unexpected loot DB writes.

Also untested: mode 2 with reason != 0 (aborted download) should emit a `Removed` progress event, not a loot record. A code change here could silently swap the two branches.

**Suggested test approach**:
Use `DownloadTracker` directly: call `append` without a prior `start` and verify it returns an appropriate error; call `finish` without `start` and verify it returns `None`. Then verify that mode 2 with non-zero reason produces no loot record in the DB.
close_reason: Added 8 unit tests verifying DownloadTracker state machine rejects out-of-order transitions: append/finish without start, double finish, append after finish, wrong agent/file IDs, and buffered byte accounting after spurious finish.

title: test: add unit tests for LootKind::label and EventKind::label
description:
**File**: `client/src/transport.rs`
**Functions with no tests**:
- `LootKind::label` (line 239) — returns display strings "Credential", "File", "Screenshot", "Other"
- `EventKind::label` (line 276) — returns display strings for filter buttons ("Agent", "Operator", "System")

**Why this matters**: `LootKind::label()` is used in `loot_item_from_flat_info` (line 1692) to derive the loot type string stored in summaries shown to operators.
