Assert that decoding fails cleanly and that ambiguous payloads do not get accepted as the wrong variant.
close_reason: Added 5 tests: missing request fields fallthrough, wrong-typed response fields rejection, empty Info rejection, direct type validation for request and response structs.

title: test: token callback parsers lack malformed-payload coverage for table subcommands
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/token.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The token dispatch tests cover the happy paths for List, privilege listing, and FindTokens, but they do not assert that malformed or truncated row data is rejected cleanly. The parsers in format_token_list, format_token_privs_list, and format_found_tokens repeatedly decode length-prefixed and UTF-16 fields until the buffer is exhausted.

Without malformed-payload tests, a partial demon packet can still produce a misleading partial table or trigger an unexpected parser failure path without any test catching it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add callback-level tests that truncate the final row for List, privilege-list mode, and FindTokens, then assert InvalidCallbackPayload is returned and no AgentResponse event is broadcast.
close_reason: Added three truncated-payload tests for token List, PrivsGetOrList (list mode), and FindTokens callbacks — each asserts InvalidCallbackPayload on malformed row data with no event broadcast

title: test: verify plugin command tasking failures do not broadcast fake success
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/plugins.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The current plugin-runtime tests focus on successful command registration and task queuing, but they do not cover the case where a registered Python command tries to queue work for an unknown or inactive agent. `queue_raw_agent_task` and `invoke_registered_command` depend on `AgentRegistry::enqueue_job`; if that fails, the runtime must return an error and avoid broadcasting an `OperatorMessage::AgentTask` that implies success.

**Suggested test approach**:
Register a test command, invoke it against an agent ID that is not present in the registry, and assert that the call returns an error and no task-broadcast event is emitted to the event bus.
close_reason: Added test invoke_command_against_unknown_agent_returns_error_and_no_broadcast: registers a plugin command, invokes it against an absent agent ID, asserts error return and no AgentTask broadcast

title: refactor: extract shared test helpers from duplicate integration test files
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs, teamserver/tests/mock_demon_agent_checkin.rs
**Gap type**: quality / maintainability

`e2e_operator_agent_session.rs` and `mock_demon_agent_checkin.rs` contain nearly identical implementations of several test helper functions:
- `login()`
- `read_operator_message()`
- `read_operator_snapshot()`
- `available_port_excluding()`
- `wait_for_listener()`
- `valid_demon_init_body()`
- `valid_demon_callback_body()`
- `add_length_prefixed_bytes_be()` / `add_length_prefixed_utf16_be()`

This means that any protocol change (e.g., new metadata fields in the demon init body or new websocket framing) requires updating both files. The duplication has already caused the two files to diverge slightly (e.g., minor differences in the `login` helper and how `error_for_status` is called). A bug introduced during copy-paste could cause one test to silently accept malformed output that the other rejects.

**Suggested approach**:
Create `teamserver/tests/common/mod.rs` with shared helpers and import them via `mod common;` in each test file. This also makes it easier to add future integration tests without re-implementing the full protocol harness.
close_reason: Created teamserver/tests/common/mod.rs with all duplicated helpers (login, login_as, read_operator_message, read_operator_snapshot, available_port_excluding, wait_for_listener, valid_demon_init_body, valid_demon_callback_body, add_length_prefixed_bytes_be/utf16_be, command_output_payload, spawn_test_server, assert_no_operator_message). Both test files updated to use mod common; All tests pass.

title: test: reject malformed base64 in AgentEncryptionInfo deserialization
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: error path

AgentRecord/AgentEncryptionInfo tests cover omission of the Encryption field and successful round trips, but they do not cover malformed base64 input for AESKey/AESIv. That means a broken database row or malformed JSON payload could regress from a clean deserialization error into partial acceptance or a panic without the test suite catching it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add deserialization tests for AgentRecord values whose Encryption.AESKey or Encryption.AESIv contain invalid base64 and assert that serde_json::from_value fails with a decode error. Include one case where only one of the two fields is malformed so both branches are exercised.
close_reason: Added 2 tests: malformed base64 in AESKey and AESIv both fail deserialization cleanly.

title: audit: unknown agent callback not recorded in audit log
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

teamserver/src/listeners.rs ~line 1378 — process_demon_transport dispatches ParsedDemonPacket::Callback packets; when decrypt_from_agent returns AgentNotFound the error is only logged at warn! level and no audit record is written. Contrast with ParsedDemonPacket::Reconnect at ~line 1362 where record_unknown_reconnect_probe is called. An attacker can repeatedly probe the listener with unknown agent IDs to fingerprint or DoS the registry with no forensic evidence.

Fix: add a record_unknown_callback_probe call in the AgentNotFound error arm, mirroring the reconnect path.
close_reason: Added record_unknown_callback_probe in the AgentNotFound error arm of process_demon_transport, mirroring the reconnect probe path. Returns Fake404 and writes an agent.callback_probe audit entry. Covered by a new end-to-end test.

title: test: cover non-download filesystem callback branches and explorer output
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/filesystem.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The current tests in `dispatch/filesystem.rs` only cover the low-level `parse_file_*` helpers, and `dispatch/mod.rs` covers download/beacon transfer flows. There is no callback-level coverage for the rest of `handle_filesystem_callback`: `Dir`, `Upload`, `Cd`, `Remove`, `Mkdir`, `Copy`, `Move`, `GetPwd`, and `Cat`.

That leaves several operator-visible behaviors unpinned: directory-list formatting in normal vs `list_only` vs explorer mode, the `No file or folder was found` fallback, `Copy`/`Move` success-vs-error message selection, and `Cat` success/failure handling. A parser offset bug or wrong message kind here would ship without a failing test because the existing helper tests never exercise the callback contract.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add async dispatcher tests that build minimal `CommandFs` payloads for each subcommand and assert the emitted `AgentResponse` kind/message/output plus any structured extras. Include one explorer-mode `Dir` case that checks `MiscType=FileExplorer` and one failing `Cat` case that verifies no file contents are attached on error.
close_reason: Added 16 async dispatcher tests covering Upload, Cd, Remove (file/dir), Mkdir, Copy/Move (success/failure), GetPwd, Cat (success/failure), Dir normal-mode formatting, and explorer-mode MiscType/MiscData assertions.

title: SMB DEMON_INIT rate limiter uses hardcoded LOCALHOST — per-IP limit non-functional for all SMB
description: In teamserver/src/listeners.rs, handle_smb_connection() assigns client_ip = IpAddr::V4(Ipv4Addr::LOCALHOST) for every incoming SMB connection before passing to DemonInitRateLimiter. This means all SMB DEMON_INIT attempts are bucketed under 127.0.0.1; the per-IP limit (5 attempts / 60 s) is shared across every SMB agent and fires after 5 total SMB DEMON_INIT requests regardless of source, while simultaneously allowing unlimited per-true-IP bypasses via parallel SMB connections. Introduced in commit 8d10163 (Co-Authored-By: Codex). Fix: extract the actual peer address from the Unix/SMB socket context (or use a synthetic IP derived from agent_id/pipe name) before passing to the rate limiter.
close_reason: derive per-agent synthetic IPv4 from frame.agent_id.to_be_bytes() in handle_smb_connection; each agent_id now gets its own rate-limit bucket instead of sharing 127.0.0.1; updated all affected tests

title: Operator credentials are stored as bare SHA3-256 digests
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp

teamserver/src/auth.rs:165 hashes profile passwords with hash_password_sha3(), and teamserver/src/auth.rs:261-267 persists runtime-created operators with the same unsalted SHA3-256 digest. The stored value is then used directly as the login verifier.

Why it matters: a database leak immediately yields fast, unsalted password digests that can be cracked offline. The current constant-time compare only protects online verification; it does not protect the persisted credential material. Because the client already submits SHA3(password) over the Havoc-compatible login protocol, the server can harden storage without breaking wire compatibility by stretching or keying that submitted digest before persistence.

Fix: store a server-side verifier such as Argon2id(hash_password_sha3(password)) or an equivalent keyed KDF output for runtime operators, and use the same hardened verifier for profile-loaded credentials in memory. Add a migration path for existing persisted runtime operators so the database no longer stores bare SHA3 digests.
close_reason: Stored Argon2id verifiers for operator SHA3 digests, upgraded legacy runtime rows on load, and added auth migration coverage

title: build_dns_txt_response hardcodes TXT QTYPE in question section regardless of original query type
description:
In listeners.rs, build_dns_txt_response reconstructs the question section of the DNS response by writing qname_raw followed by DNS_TYPE_TXT (16) and DNS_CLASS_IN. However, the original query's QTYPE is not passed in, so if the query was an A record query (QTYPE=1), the response question section claims QTYPE=TXT (16), which violates RFC 1035 §4.1.2 (the question section in a response must echo the original question).

The ParsedDnsQuery struct already contains a qtype field. The fix is to add a qtype parameter to build_dns_txt_response (or pass the full ParsedDnsQuery) and use query.qtype instead of DNS_TYPE_TXT when writing the question section.

Priority 3 — this is a protocol correctness issue but doesn't affect C2 functionality since all C2 traffic uses TXT queries, and the current code always responds with a TXT answer regardless.
close_reason: Preserved the original DNS query QTYPE when rebuilding TXT response questions and added a regression test.

title: test: add direct unit coverage for basename and process_arch_label helpers
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/util.rs
**Gap type**: existence

`windows_version_label` and `windows_arch_label` are exercised indirectly, but `basename()` and `process_arch_label()` currently have no direct tests even though both feed operator-visible agent metadata in the INIT and CHECKIN paths. A regression here would quietly corrupt process names or architecture labels without tripping a focused assertion.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add small unit tests for Windows and POSIX path inputs, trailing separators, and unknown architecture values. Assert `basename()` returns the final component and `process_arch_label()` maps x86, x64, IA64, and unknown values exactly as expected.
close_reason: Tests already exist: basename has 9 direct unit tests covering empty, bare filename, Windows/POSIX/mixed paths, trailing separators; process_arch_label has 2 tests covering all known arch constants and unknown fallback values. All pass.

title: test: authenticate_message with completely invalid JSON returns InvalidMessageJson error
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/auth.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `authenticate_message` method has two distinct error paths:
1. Valid JSON but non-Login message → `Err(AuthError::InvalidLoginMessage)`
2. Invalid/unparseable JSON → `Err(AuthError::InvalidMessageJson(...))`

Path 1 is tested in `authenticate_message_rejects_non_login_messages`, but path 2 is completely untested. Without a test, a future refactor could silently collapse both paths into the same error or accidentally panic on malformed input — a security-relevant regression since this is the outer gate for operator connections.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `authenticate_message` with an input like `"not-valid-json{"` and assert the result is `Err(AuthError::InvalidMessageJson(_))`. Confirm no session is created and no panic occurs.
close_reason: Added authenticate_message_rejects_invalid_json test: passes invalid JSON and asserts Err(InvalidMessageJson(_)) with no session created

title: test: add unit coverage for AuditResultStatus::as_str
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/audit.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn as_str` (line 27) — returns the stable serialized status label used when building audit filters and payloads.

**Why this matters**: Audit status filtering depends on a stable string mapping. A regression here could silently break `result_status` filtering or serialized webhook/database output without failing broader integration tests.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: `AuditResultStatus::Success.as_str()` returns `"success"`.
2. Happy path: `AuditResultStatus::Failure.as_str()` returns `"failure"`.
3. Edge case: assert the mapping is lowercase and stable for both enum variants so future renames cannot change the wire value accidentally.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/audit.rs`
close_reason: Added three inline unit tests: as_str_success, as_str_failure, and as_str_is_lowercase_and_stable covering both enum variants and wire-label stability.

title: test: agent_mark_event Alive case not covered — only Dead path is tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_events.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`agent_mark_event` returns `"Alive"` when `agent.active == true` and `"Dead"` when false. The existing test `agent_mark_event_uses_dead_status_for_inactive_agents` covers only the Dead branch. The Alive branch is untested.

This is a simple two-branch function and the Alive path is the one broadcast when an agent that was previously considered dead reconnects — a regression here could silently mark a recovered agent as Dead in operator consoles.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `agent_mark_event` with an agent where `active = true`, assert the resulting `OperatorMessage::AgentUpdate` has `marked == "Alive"`.
close_reason: Added unit coverage for the Alive branch in agent_mark_event.

title: consistency: DemonInitRateLimiter and LoginRateLimiter duplicate eviction logic verbatim
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com> (rate limiter implementation)

teamserver/src/listeners.rs and teamserver/src/websocket.rs both implement a sliding-window rate limiter with identical eviction logic: MAX_*_ATTEMPT_WINDOWS = 10_000, evict_oldest_* functions that remove the oldest half of entries when the cap is reached.

The duplicated code means a bug fix or policy change in one eviction path will not automatically apply to the other. Both currently use the same cap and strategy with no documented reason for the divergence.

Fix: extract a generic SlidingWindowRateLimiter<K> type (or at minimum a shared evict_oldest_windows() utility function) into a shared module and have both usages delegate to it.
close_reason: extracted AttemptWindow, evict_oldest_windows, and prune_expired_windows into teamserver/src/rate_limiter.rs; both DemonInitRateLimiter and LoginRateLimiter now delegate to the shared functions

title: test: database.rs LootFilter and AuditLogFilter dynamic SQL query logic untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs
**Gap type**: existence | edge case

`LootRepository::query_filtered` (line 985) and `AuditLogRepository::query_filtered` (line 1218) build dynamic SQL WHERE clauses from filter structs. The only inline tests in database.rs cover `bool_from_i64` and `i64_from_u64` (3 tests total).

The filter logic dynamically appends conditions for agent_id, category, operator, date ranges, etc. Edge cases that could produce incorrect SQL:
- Empty filter (no conditions) should return all records
- Multiple conditions combined with AND
- Date range with only start or only end specified
- agent_id filter with hex parsing

A SQL injection or incorrect WHERE clause assembly would silently return wrong data to operators reviewing loot or audit logs.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create an in-memory database, insert known records with varying agent_ids/categories/dates, then call `query_filtered` with different filter combinations and assert the correct subset is returned. Test `count_filtered` returns matching counts.
close_reason: Superseded: teamserver/tests/database.rs now includes extensive LootFilter and AuditLogFilter tests — count_filtered, query_filtered with multi-field filters, pagination, date ranges, agent_id + kind combined filters, operator_contains on JSON metadata, and empty-range edge cases. All gaps described in this issue are now covered.

title: test: add constructor coverage for PayloadBuilderService::from_profile
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn from_profile` (line 149) — resolves the MinGW/NASM toolchain and required Havoc payload asset paths from the loaded profile.

**Why this matters**: this is the public constructor for payload building. If it accepts a broken toolchain configuration or mis-resolves repository assets, payload generation will fail at runtime after operator requests are already in flight.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: resolve a profile whose build settings point to usable executables and existing payload assets.
2. Error path: reject a profile with a missing compiler or NASM binary and assert `PayloadBuildError::ToolchainUnavailable`.
3. Edge case: reject missing Havoc payload asset files even when the toolchain paths are valid.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs`
close_reason: Added constructor coverage for successful toolchain resolution, missing binaries, and missing Havoc payload assets.

title: test: load_tls_identity error paths (missing cert, missing key, corrupt PEM) not tested
description:
**File**: common/src/tls.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`load_tls_identity` returns three error variants that have no test coverage:
- `TlsError::MissingCertificates` when the PEM buffer contains no X.509 certificate blocks
- `TlsError::MissingPrivateKey` when the PEM buffer contains no private key block
- `TlsError::Pem` when the PEM data is completely corrupt/binary

`load_tls_identity_from_files` also has an untested `TlsError::ReadFile` path (non-existent paths). The existing round-trip test only exercises the happy path.

Since TLS identity loading is on the critical path for listener startup and a malformed cert file should produce a clear error rather than a panic or silent failure, these gaps matter.

**Suggested test approach**:
Pass empty bytes to `load_tls_identity` (→ MissingCertificates), pass a valid cert PEM but empty key (→ MissingPrivateKey), call `load_tls_identity_from_files` with a non-existent path (→ ReadFile), and assert the correct `TlsError` variant in each case.
close_reason: Added TLS identity loader tests for missing cert, missing key, corrupt PEM, and missing file errors.

title: test: startup helper only covers HTTP profile listeners
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/main.rs
**Gap type**: integration / existence

`start_new_profile_listeners` is only exercised with HTTP listeners in `main.rs` tests. The helper is part of the real boot path and is responsible for auto-starting newly synced listeners from the loaded profile, but there is no test proving it does the same for SMB or DNS listeners.

This matters because the startup filter in `profile_listener_names` and the `Created -> Running` transition in `start_new_profile_listeners` are exactly where non-HTTP listener types can be silently skipped during boot. Lower-level runtime tests in `listeners.rs` would still pass even if `main.rs` stopped auto-starting DNS or SMB profile listeners.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add `main.rs` startup tests that sync a profile containing a DNS listener and a profile containing an SMB listener, call `start_new_profile_listeners`, and assert each listener ends in `Running` state rather than being left `Created` or ignored.
close_reason: Added startup_auto_starts_new_smb_profile_listener and startup_auto_starts_new_dns_profile_listener tests to main.rs, covering the SMB and DNS listener types that were missing from start_new_profile_listeners coverage

title: test: reject callback packets for unregistered agents without advancing Demon CTR state
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/demon.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`DemonPacketParser::parse_at_for_listener` decrypts callback traffic through the registry and only advances CTR after a successful parse. Existing tests cover garbage payloads for registered agents, but there is no explicit test for callbacks that target an agent ID the registry does not know about. That blind spot matters because an attacker can send arbitrary callback envelopes with guessed IDs; the parser must return the registry error cleanly and avoid mutating any stored keystream state.

**Suggested test approach**:
Build a well-formed callback envelope for an unregistered agent ID, call the parser, and assert it returns the registry/agent-not-found error. Also assert no agent was inserted and no CTR state was created as a side effect.
close_reason: Added test callback_for_unregistered_agent_returns_not_found_without_creating_state in demon.rs: asserts AgentNotFound error, no agent insertion, and no CTR state creation for callbacks targeting unknown agent IDs

title: chore: close 35dz — tests were committed but issue left in_progress after interruption
description:
Introduced by: Claude (dev02-opus)

Issue red-cell-c2-35dz (LootRepository::get and list tests) was fully implemented in commit 669b210 but left in_progress because the dev session was interrupted (wip: interrupted red-cell-c2-35dz). The three requested test scenarios are all present in teamserver/tests/database.rs. Issue just needs to be closed.
close_reason: Housekeeping — the stuck 35dz issue has been resolved by prior cleanup

title: handle_token_callback returns error for all subcommands except Impersonate
description: crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs handle_token_callback (registered for COMMAND_TOKEN) only handles DemonTokenCommand::Impersonate and propagates a CommandDispatchError for every other subcommand (Steal=2, List=3, PrivsGetOrList=4, Make=5, GetUid=6, Revert=7, Remove=8, Clear=9, FindTokens=10). When the Demon agent sends back a callback for any of those subcommands, the handler fails, causing the response to be silently dropped (the error is logged but no operator event is emitted). Either implement the remaining subcommands or handle them with a generic/no-op response rather than returning an error that kills the callback.
close_reason: Implemented all token subcommand callbacks (Steal, List, PrivsGetOrList, Make, GetUid, Revert, Remove, Clear, FindTokens) with protocol-compatible parsing matching the Havoc Demon agent. Also updated encode_token_payload and token_subcommand to support all subcommands from the operator side. Added 12 tests covering all subcommands.

title: test: deserialize_bool_from_any rejects unrecognized string values like "yes" or "maybe"
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`deserialize_bool_from_any` accepts the strings `"true"`, `"1"`, `"false"`, `"0"`, and `""` — all other strings fall into the error branch: `Err(de::Error::custom(format!("invalid boolean value `{other}`")))`. This branch has no test coverage.

There is an existing open issue (red-cell-c2-3rio) for the *integer* case (values outside {0,1}), but the *string* case is distinct. Fields like `active` and `elevated` on `AgentRecord` use this deserializer. If a malformed Havoc message arrived with a value like `"yes"` or `"Active"`, the deserializer should cleanly reject it rather than silently produce a wrong value.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `serde_json::from_value::<AgentRecord>` (or directly test the helper via a JSON-level test) with `"Active": "yes"` or `"Elevated": "maybe"` and assert that deserialization fails with an error message containing `"invalid boolean value"`.
close_reason: Added two tests: rejects 'yes' on Active and 'maybe' on Elevated, both asserting error message contains 'invalid boolean value'

title: Auth Argon2 hashing in test profile setup makes full test suite unfeasibly slow
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/auth.rs:156-163 — AuthService::from_profile now calls password_verifier_for_sha3 (which runs Argon2::default().hash_password) for every configured profile operator plus the dummy verifier. Previously this was a single fast SHA3 call.

Every test that calls AuthService::from_profile in its setup now incurs N+1 Argon2 hashes (N profile operators + 1 dummy). The standard test profile has 2 operators, so each from_profile call does 3 Argon2 operations. At 19MB memory cost and 2 iterations per hash, this adds ~1-2 seconds per test setup. Auth, API, websocket, rbac, and main tests all call from_profile, meaning hundreds of Argon2 operations run during the full suite.

Observed: cargo test --workspace with all 360 tests times out consistently (>120s). Individual module filters pass (auth: 23s for 18 tests, api: 8s for 50 tests with 4 threads), but running the entire suite is impractical.

Correct behavior: Test setups should use a fast in-memory credential path (e.g., pre-computed test verifiers via once_cell/lazy_static, or a test-only constructor that skips Argon2 and stores a sentinel that compare matches). Production from_profile should remain hardened.
close_reason: Added cfg(test) OnceLock cache in password_verifier_for_sha3: each unique SHA3 input is hashed via Argon2 at most once per test process. Full suite now completes in ~16s runtime vs previous timeout >120s. Production path unchanged.

title: test: add unit coverage for dispatch util path and arch helpers
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/util.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `basename` (line 66) — strips directory prefixes from Windows or POSIX paths before agent records are updated on INIT/CHECKIN.
- `process_arch_label` (line 71) — maps Demon `process_arch` numeric values into the operator-facing architecture strings stored on agent records.

**Why this matters**: these helpers sit on the agent metadata path used by both DEMON_INIT and CHECKIN. A regression here would silently mislabel process names or architectures in the UI and database while broader integration tests still pass because they do not assert these helper branches directly.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: `basename` returns the trailing component for Windows (`C:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe`) and POSIX (`/usr/bin/ssh`) paths.
2. Edge case: `basename` handles bare filenames, trailing separators, and mixed slash styles without panicking.
3. Mapping coverage: `process_arch_label` returns `x86`, `x64`, and `IA64` for the known constants and `Unknown` for an unrecognized value.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/dispatch/util.rs`
close_reason: Tests already exist inline in teamserver/src/dispatch/util.rs — 9 tests for basename (Windows/POSIX/edge cases) and 2 tests for process_arch_label (known values + unknown fallback). All 18 util tests pass.

title: DNS listener response map (DnsPendingResponse) has no expiry, causing unbounded memory growth
description:
In listeners.rs, DnsListenerState contains a 'responses: Mutex<HashMap<u32, DnsPendingResponse>>' that is populated when an agent upload is processed successfully. Entries are never removed: handle_download returns 'done' when the agent has polled all chunks but does not call responses.remove(&agent_id). Under active C2 use, every completed agent interaction leaves a stale entry in this map.

By contrast, the uploads map does expire entries via 'uploads.retain(|_, p| p.received_at.elapsed().as_secs() < DNS_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS)' called in try_assemble_upload.

Fix: either (a) remove the entry from responses inside handle_download when idx >= total (converting &self to &mut self or using interior mutability properly), or (b) add a similar time-based expiry by adding a received_at timestamp to DnsPendingResponse and purging in handle_download or a background cleanup task.
close_reason: Removed completed DNS pending responses on terminal download polls and added a regression test.

title: test: ShutdownController lacks coverage for pre-shutdown waiters waking on initiate
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/shutdown.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`ShutdownController::notified()` is only tested when shutdown has already started. There is no test that creates one or more waiter tasks before `initiate()`, then asserts they all unblock after shutdown begins. Without that coverage, a regression in the notify ordering or the early-return check could leave listener/runtime tasks hung during shutdown even though the current tests still pass.

**Suggested test approach**:
Spawn multiple tasks awaiting `controller.notified()`, assert they remain pending before shutdown, call `initiate()`, then verify every waiter completes within a tight timeout and that repeated `initiate()` calls do not break wake-up semantics.
close_reason: Added 2 tests: 5 pre-shutdown waiters all wake on initiate(), and repeated initiate() calls are idempotent.

title: test: logging config does not cover default and minutely file rotation
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/logging.rs
**Gap type**: quality / edge case

The logging tests cover filter selection and one explicit `Hourly` file rotation, but they never assert two important branches in the file-logging config path: the default `Daily` rotation when the profile omits `Rotation`, and the `Minutely` mapping used by `map_rotation`.

If either branch regressed, startup would still succeed and the existing tests would remain green, but log files would rotate on the wrong schedule in production. That is exactly the kind of silent operational bug quality tests should catch.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add unit coverage around `resolve_logging_config_with_override` for an omitted `Rotation` field and an explicit `Rotation = \"Minutely\"`. Assert the resolved file config uses `Daily` by default and preserves `Minutely` when configured.
close_reason: Added unit tests for default Daily rotation (omitted Rotation field) and explicit Minutely rotation in resolve_logging_config_with_override

title: No end-to-end integration test: HTTP listener → parser → dispatcher pipeline
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/tests/ contains only database.rs. There is no integration test that exercises the full path: HTTP listener receives a Demon packet → DemonPacketParser parses it → CommandDispatcher routes the callback → operator event is broadcast.

All components are unit-tested in isolation, but a wiring error (e.g., the listener passing the wrong field as external_ip, or the dispatcher failing to find the agent) would not be caught.

Fix: add an integration test that starts a real HTTP listener on a loopback port, sends a synthesized DEMON_INIT followed by a COMMAND_CHECKIN, and asserts that the agent appears in the registry and the correct operator event is broadcast.
close_reason: Added a real HTTP listener integration test covering DEMON_INIT registration, registry state, and COMMAND_CHECKIN event dispatch.

title: test: screenshot callback rejects truncated byte payloads without emitting loot events
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/screenshot_dispatch.rs
**Gap type**: round-trip

`handle_screenshot_callback` in `teamserver/src/dispatch/screenshot.rs` parses a `success` flag followed by a length-prefixed byte buffer, but the current integration tests only cover `success=0`, empty bytes, and a valid success case. There is no test for malformed callback bodies where the declared screenshot length is larger than the bytes actually present or the payload stops immediately after the success flag. Without this, a parser regression could panic, partially persist loot, or emit a misleading operator event on malformed Demon traffic.

**Suggested test approach**:
Send a `CommandScreenshot` callback whose byte-length prefix overstates the available bytes, and another that truncates immediately after `success=1`. Assert the HTTP response is non-2xx, no loot row is stored, and no `AgentResponse` / `loot-new` event is broadcast.
close_reason: Added 2 integration tests: truncated-after-success-flag and overstated-length payloads. Both verify non-2xx response, no loot stored, no success events.

title: test: BOF_CALLBACK_ERROR (0x0d) sub-type in handle_inline_execute_callback is the only untested BOF path
description:
File: teamserver/src/dispatch/assembly.rs
Gap type: error path / edge case

The handle_inline_execute_callback function dispatches 6 BOF sub-types. Five are covered by the inline_execute_bof_output_broadcasts_agent_response test in dispatch/mod.rs (BOF_CALLBACK_OUTPUT=0x00, BOF_EXCEPTION=1, BOF_SYMBOL_NOT_FOUND=2, BOF_RAN_OK=3, BOF_COULD_NOT_RUN=4). The BOF_CALLBACK_ERROR sub-type (0x0d) — standard error text output from a BOF — has no test.

This means a regression in error output parsing (e.g., reading the wrong number of bytes for the error string length) would produce silent garbage in operator consoles without any test catching it. The 0x0d constant value is also non-obvious and a transcription error would be invisible without a test.

Suggested test approach:
Build a payload with sub-type 0x0d followed by a length-prefixed error string, dispatch it through CommandDispatcher::with_builtin_handlers, and assert the broadcast event has Type=Error and the expected message content.
close_reason: Added unit tests for BOF_CALLBACK_ERROR (0x0d) sub-type: happy-path broadcast assertion (Type=Error + message content), truncated payload error path, and integration test through CommandDispatcher

title: parse_checkin_metadata silently saturates working_hours to i32::MAX on overflow
description:
Introduced by: Codex (commit 983789a - fix: refresh metadata on demon checkin)

In `teamserver/src/dispatch.rs`, the new `parse_checkin_metadata` function converts the protocol `working_hours` field (u32) to `Option<i32>` using `i32::try_from(working_hours).unwrap_or(i32::MAX)` (line ~858). Any agent sending a working_hours bitmask with the high bit set (e.g. 0x80000000–0xFFFFFFFF) will silently have its schedule replaced with i32::MAX rather than being rejected or stored correctly.

This is the same silent-saturation pattern already documented in red-cell-c2-2x0k (DEMON_INIT path). That bug is unresolved, and the new COMMAND_CHECKIN path (which can rotate the working_hours schedule mid-session) now inherits the same defect.

Correct behaviour: store working_hours as u32 to match the protocol, or reject out-of-range values with an explicit error, consistent with whatever fix is applied to red-cell-c2-2x0k.
close_reason: Preserved raw working-hours bitmasks for check-in and config updates, with regression coverage for high-bit values.

title: axum::serve errors silently swallowed in webhook and audit test helpers
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/webhook.rs line 519 and teamserver/src/audit.rs lines 813 and 830 use let _ = axum::serve(listener, app).await inside spawned Tokio tasks. If the mock HTTP server fails to bind or exits with an error, the error is silently discarded. This means test-helper server failures will cause dependent tests to time out or produce confusing assertion errors with no indication of root cause.

Fix: replace with .expect("test webhook server should not fail") or log the error with tracing::error! so failures are immediately visible.
close_reason: Replaced let _ = axum::serve(...).await with .expect() in webhook.rs, audit.rs (both helpers), and websocket.rs so test server failures are immediately visible

title: test: EventBus history ring buffer eviction at capacity=1 is untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/events.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / boundary condition

The existing `zero_capacity_history_does_not_retain_logs` and `recent_teamserver_logs_retain_only_log_events` tests cover capacity=0 and capacity=2. The boundary case **capacity=1** exercises the degenerate path where every new log message immediately evicts the only existing entry.

Two specific sub-cases are missing:

1. **capacity=1, second log evicts first**: broadcast two TeamserverLog events on a bus with capacity=1; `recent_teamserver_logs()` must return only the second message. Currently the ring-buffer logic at line 59 (`if history.len() == self.history_capacity { history.pop_front(); }`) is exercised by the capacity=2 test but the single-slot case has a different memory access pattern that is easy to get wrong (e.g. pop-before-push vs push-before-pop).

2. **Wrap-around preserves ordering**: for capacity=N, broadcast N+2 messages and assert the returned slice is the last N messages in correct chronological order. The capacity=2 test only checks that old messages are evicted but not that the order is preserved across multiple evictions.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add two `#[tokio::test]` cases: one with capacity=1 that broadcasts two messages and asserts only the second is retained; one with capacity=3 that broadcasts five messages and asserts the last three are returned in order.
close_reason: Added two tests: capacity_one_evicts_on_second_log and wraparound_preserves_chronological_order covering the single-slot eviction edge case and multi-eviction ordering

title: test: cover truncated SOCKS5 negotiation and CONNECT request parsing in socket relay
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: error path

 and  are exercised for wrong versions, unsupported commands, and unknown ATYP values, but there is no test for truncated client input (short greeting, missing METHODS bytes, partial IPv4/IPv6/domain address, or missing port bytes).

This is protocol-parsing code on an unauthenticated local socket boundary. Without truncated-input tests, a refactor could accidentally block, panic, or emit the wrong SOCKS reply when the client disconnects mid-handshake.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add focused async tests that send each malformed short frame over a real , then assert the function returns an  and, where applicable, that the server does not report a success reply or retain client state.
close_reason: Added empty-stream and partial-port truncation tests for SOCKS5 negotiate and connect request parsing, complementing the existing truncated-input tests (version-only, short method list, truncated header/IPv4/IPv6/domain, missing port). All 32 sockets unit tests pass.

title: quality: no WebSocket max message size configured — authenticated operator can force 64 MiB allocation
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp (Claude Sonnet 4.6)

teamserver/src/websocket.rs — No explicit max WebSocket message size is set on the
axum/tungstenite connection. The tungstenite default is 64 MiB per frame. A connected
(authenticated) operator could send an oversized frame and force a ~64 MiB allocation
server-side.

Severity is moderate because it requires valid operator credentials. In a multi-tenant
deployment where operators have different trust levels, a lower-privilege operator
(Analyst role) could still cause this allocation.

Fix: configure the WebSocket connection with a sensible max message size (e.g. 1 MiB,
matching typical operator command payloads) using the axum WebSocketUpgrade::max_message_size
method.
close_reason: Configured a 1 MiB websocket max message size and added regression coverage for oversized frames.

title: test: add_link error path for nonexistent parent or child agent untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agents.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`add_link(parent_agent_id, link_agent_id)` returns `TeamserverError::AgentNotFound` when either the parent or child agent ID does not exist in the registry (lines 722-727). This error path has no dedicated test. Existing `add_link` tests only cover self-link, cycle detection, and depth limits — they all use pre-registered agents.

Without this test, a refactor that accidentally removes the existence check (e.g. skipping the `.ok_or()` guard) would not be caught.

**Suggested test approach**:
Insert a single agent, then call `add_link(known_id, unknown_id)` and `add_link(unknown_id, known_id)`. Assert both return `Err(TeamserverError::AgentNotFound { .. })` with the correct `agent_id` in the error.
close_reason: Added two tests: add_link_rejects_nonexistent_parent and add_link_rejects_nonexistent_child covering the AgentNotFound error path when either parent or child agent ID is missing from the registry.

title: security: shutdown race in AuditWebhookNotifier allows webhook delivery after shutdown completes
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/webhook.rs — notify_audit_record_detached checks closing.load() then increments pending.fetch_add(). Between these two operations, shutdown() can observe pending==0 and return true (claiming all deliveries are drained) before the new spawn increments the counter. The spawned task then runs after the caller believes shutdown is complete.

While the Arc'd reqwest client prevents a use-after-free, the shutdown guarantee ('returns true only when all deliveries are complete') is violated. Any resource teardown that depends on this guarantee is unsound.

Fix: use a compare-and-swap approach: attempt to increment pending first, then check whether closing was set concurrently; if so, decrement and discard.
close_reason: Reversed the check order in notify_audit_record_detached: increment pending first, then check closing; if closing was set concurrently, decrement and wake shutdown. Regression test added.

title: bug: stale compiled test socks_proxy_add_is_noop causes intermittent test failures
description:
Introduced by: Claude (Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.6)

A ghost test dispatch::socket::tests::socks_proxy_add_is_noop appears during full cargo test runs but does not exist in any source file. It was likely removed from dispatch/socket.rs but its compiled artifact remains in target/. The test panics at socket.rs:728 with assertion failed: result.is_ok().

Symptoms:
- Full `cargo test --workspace` shows 1 failure from this ghost test
- Running the test by name (`cargo test -- socks_proxy_add_is_noop`) matches 0 tests
- The test passes on clean builds after `cargo clean`

Fix: Run `cargo clean` to purge stale artifacts, or verify that the test's source was properly removed and the dispatch/socket.rs module compiles cleanly from scratch.
close_reason: stale: test socks_proxy_add_is_noop does not exist in the codebase. Existing socks proxy tests cover success, failure, and truncated payload scenarios.

title: Session token lookup is not constant-time (timing oracle)
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/auth.rs — SessionRegistry::get_by_token() at line 394 uses BTreeMap::get() which performs a standard string comparison. String comparison is not constant-time in Rust: it short-circuits on the first unequal byte. An attacker who can observe response latency with high precision (e.g. via a side-channel over a local network) can use this to enumerate valid session tokens byte by byte. Fix: replace the BTreeMap token lookup with a linear scan using subtle::ConstantTimeEq or a pre-hashed token digest keyed by HMAC-SHA256 so that mismatched tokens produce equal-length fixed-size comparison operands.
close_reason: Replaced BTreeMap::get token lookup with constant-time linear scan using subtle::ConstantTimeEq; added three regression tests

title: No cap on agent registry size — unauthenticated DEMON_INIT flood exhausts memory and SQLite
description:
Introduced by: Codex (original agent registry design)

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs — AgentRegistry.entries (HashMap<u32, Arc<AgentEntry>>) has no maximum capacity. Every DEMON_INIT received by an HTTP, SMB, or DNS listener inserts a new entry and a corresponding SQLite row (via database::AgentRepository::create).

An attacker who can reach the agent-facing listener port (which is intentionally internet-exposed for real implants) can flood DEMON_INIT packets with distinct agent_id values (u32 → 4 billion distinct IDs). Each fake registration:
- allocates an AgentEntry with its own RwLock, Mutex, VecDeque, and HashMap
- persists an agents row + encryption row to SQLite
- broadcasts an AgentNew event to all connected operators

Combined with the zero-key bypass (red-cell-c2-2d4), crafting valid registration packets requires no crypto knowledge. Without a rate limit or max-agent cap, a single attacker machine can OOM the teamserver in minutes.

Fix: add a configurable MAX_REGISTERED_AGENTS limit (suggested default: 10 000) to AgentRegistry::insert, returning an error when exceeded. Also consider per-IP rate limiting on DEMON_INIT acceptance (distinct from the operator login rate limiter).
close_reason: Added configurable agent registry cap enforcement on insert and startup load, with regression coverage for both DEMON_INIT and persisted-agent overflow paths.

title: Payload Builder - emit DotNetNamePipe in Demon config packing
description: The payload builder parses Demon.DotNetNamePipe from YAOTL profiles but never includes it when packing the build configuration for generated Demon payloads. Implement the remaining config packing needed for .NET output transport compatibility and add tests covering profile defaults and wire layout.
close_reason: Packed DotNetNamePipe into Demon build config bytes and added profile-default plus wire-layout regression tests.

title: test: enforce Python tab error paths for empty titles and cross-script layout changes
description:
**File**: client/src/python.rs
**Gap type**: error path

, , and  enforce several isolation rules: tab titles cannot be empty after trimming, layouts cannot be set for tabs that were never created, and one script cannot mutate another script's tab layout. The current tests only prove the happy path for a single script-owned tab. Without negative tests, a refactor could weaken these checks and let one plugin clobber another plugin's UI state without detection.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create small scripts that try to , call  before creating a tab, and have a second script mutate the first script's tab. Assert each path returns the expected  and leaves the registered tab set unchanged.
close_reason: Added three negative tests covering empty tab title rejection, SetTabLayout on uncreated tab, and cross-script tab layout mutation isolation

title: bug: kill_date u64→i64 overflow silently disables kill date enforcement
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/demon.rs:319

```rust
kill_date: (kill_date != 0).then_some(i64::try_from(kill_date).unwrap_or(i64::MAX)),
```

## What is wrong

The Demon INIT payload carries kill_date as a u64. Any value greater than i64::MAX
(9,223,372,036,854,775,807 — roughly year 292 million) is silently converted to i64::MAX
instead of returning an error or clamping to a meaningful sentinel. In practice, a Demon
compiled with a malformed kill_date field (e.g., 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFF) would be registered
with kill_date = i64::MAX, which is effectively 'never expire'. The kill_date is never
enforced so the agent runs indefinitely past any intended expiration.

## Why it matters

Kill dates are an operational security control. Silently disabling them for agents that report
out-of-range kill_date values is a correctness bug that could leave implants running longer
than authorized.

## Fix

Return DemonParserError::InvalidInit for kill_date values that do not fit in i64, or clamp to
i64::MAX with a warning log. At minimum, add a test for kill_date > i64::MAX.
close_reason: Rejected out-of-range kill_date values in demon init, checkin metadata, and config callbacks with regression coverage.

title: BehindRedirector trusts spoofable client IP headers
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2239-2258 accepts X-Forwarded-For and X-Real-IP from any peer whenever behind_redirector is enabled. crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2154-2172 then feeds that derived IP into DEMON_INIT rate limiting and persists it as the agent's ExternalIP.

There is no trusted-proxy allowlist or peer verification, so any client that can reach the listener directly can spoof arbitrary source IPs, bypass the per-IP DEMON_INIT limiter, and poison operator-visible attribution. The fix is to only trust forwarding headers from explicitly configured proxy peers/networks, otherwise fall back to the actual socket peer address.
close_reason: Only trust forwarded client IP headers from explicitly configured proxy IPs/CIDRs; otherwise use the socket peer address.

title: havoc_compatibility test is tied to an absolute checkout path and Go toolchain
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/tests/havoc_compatibility.rs:260-269 and teamserver/tests/havoc_compatibility.rs:333-342 make the new workspace test depend on `go run` plus a hardcoded replace path of `/home/michel/Red-Cell-C2/src/Havoc/teamserver`.

This passes only on machines that use this exact checkout path and have Go installed. On any other checkout location, or on Rust-only CI/dev environments without Go, `cargo test --workspace` will fail before the Havoc compatibility assertion even runs.

Correct behavior: resolve the Havoc reference directory relative to the repo at runtime (or via an environment/config hook), and either document/gate the Go dependency or skip the test cleanly when the prerequisite toolchain is unavailable.
close_reason: Resolved the Havoc test path dynamically and skip cleanly when Go or the reference checkout is unavailable

title: test: reject malformed transfer, mem-file, and package-dropped callbacks cleanly
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/transfer.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The transfer callback handlers currently have happy-path coverage for the `List`, `CommandMemFile`, and `CommandPackageDropped` paths, but no malformed-payload coverage. These handlers parse attacker-controlled Demon callback bytes such as subcommands, booleans, IDs, and lengths, and they should return structured errors instead of panicking or emitting misleading operator events when payloads are truncated or contain unknown subcommands.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add dispatch-level tests for truncated `CommandTransfer` payloads (missing subcommand or missing fields for `List`, `Stop`, `Resume`, or `Remove`), invalid transfer subcommand values, truncated `CommandMemFile` payloads, and truncated `CommandPackageDropped` payloads. Assert dispatch returns an error and that no success event is broadcast for malformed inputs.
close_reason: Added 11 malformed-payload tests for CommandTransfer (invalid subcommand, truncated List/Stop/Resume/Remove), CommandMemFile (empty, truncated), and CommandPackageDropped (empty, truncated). All assert InvalidCallbackPayload error and no event broadcast.

title: test: verify filesystem download close-failure and invalid-mode handling
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/filesystem.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`handle_filesystem_callback` covers successful filesystem downloads in dispatcher tests, but there is no test for close reason `!= 0` or for unsupported download modes. That leaves two important behaviors unverified: failed downloads should emit a `Removed` progress event without persisting loot, and malformed mode values should return `InvalidCallbackPayload` without leaving partial state behind.

**Suggested test approach**:
Open a download, append one chunk, then send a close payload with a non-zero reason and assert the tracker is drained, no loot row is stored, and the final event reports `State="Removed"`. Add a second test that dispatches an unsupported mode and asserts the error plus absence of new events or active download state.
close_reason: Added tests for download close-failure (non-zero reason emits Removed, no loot persisted, tracker drained) and invalid download mode (returns InvalidCallbackPayload, no events or partial state)

title: test: plugin event emission hooks (emit_agent_checkin, emit_command_output, etc.) have zero test coverage
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/plugins.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The `PluginRuntime` struct exposes 6 event emission methods (`emit_agent_checkin`, `emit_command_output`, `emit_agent_registered`, `emit_agent_dead`, `emit_loot_captured`, `emit_task_created`) that dispatch into embedded Python callbacks. None of these have any test coverage at all — the entire plugins.rs file has zero `#[cfg(test)]` module.

These are security-relevant: a malicious or buggy plugin could silently swallow events, crash the runtime via Python exceptions, or cause mutex poisoning that cascades to other threads. Without tests, regressions in the Python↔Rust bridge (e.g. wrong argument marshalling, missing GIL acquisition) would go undetected.

Existing issues red-cell-c2-vka4 and red-cell-c2-q5u8 cover `load_script` and multi-plugin isolation respectively, but event emission is a separate untested surface.

**Suggested test approach**:
Initialize a `PluginRuntime` with a test Python script that records callback invocations into a Python list, then call each `emit_*` method and assert the list contains the expected entries. Also test that a Python exception in a callback returns `Err(PluginError::Python)` rather than panicking.
close_reason: All 6 emit_* methods already have comprehensive test coverage: callback invocation, unknown-agent skip, exception isolation, and no-callback silence tests all exist and pass (41 tests in plugins::tests module).

title: test: deserialize_u16_from_any port-overflow rejection path is untested
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / error path

`deserialize_u16_from_any` (and `deserialize_optional_u16_from_any`) delegate to `u16::try_from` and return an error for values > 65535. This is used for `port_bind` and `port_conn` on every `HttpListenerConfig` and `HttpListenerProxyConfig`.

No test supplies a port value outside [0, 65535] and asserts the rejection. A broken or removed bounds check would silently truncate large port numbers without any test catching it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct an `HttpListenerConfig` JSON payload with `"port_bind": 70000` (a string or integer) and assert `serde_json::from_value` returns an error. Similarly test `"port": "99999"` in the proxy config.
close_reason: Added three tests covering port overflow rejection: port_bind integer 70000, port_conn string 99999, and proxy port string 99999 all assert the 'does not fit in u16' error

title: test: cover listener manager same-port conflicts between managed listeners
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/listener_lifecycle.rs
**Gap type**: error path

listener_lifecycle.rs verifies failure when an external process already owns a port, but it does not cover the case where two persisted listeners are configured with the same bind port and the second start collides with the first.

Without that integration test, a state-management bug could mark both listeners as running, lose the original listener state, or fail to persist last_error for the colliding listener.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create two HTTP listeners with different names but the same bind port. Start the first successfully, then start the second and assert it returns an error, persists ListenerStatus::Error plus last_error, and leaves the first listener running and reachable.
close_reason: Added integration test covering same-port conflict between two managed listeners: second start fails with Error status and last_error, first listener stays Running and reachable

title: test: no test for AES-CTR key-rotation boundary in handle_checkin
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:780-782 — The checkin path that replaces an agent's AES key+IV
and resets the CTR offset to 0 has no dedicated unit test. The test suite covers the
unknown-agent checkin error path but not the happy path for key rotation.

Without a test that: (1) registers an agent with key A, (2) sends some encrypted traffic
advancing the CTR offset, (3) sends a checkin with key B resetting the offset, and (4)
verifies subsequent traffic is encrypted with key B at offset 0 — it is easy to introduce
a keystream-reuse regression (same key+IV+offset used for two different plaintexts).

This is the most security-sensitive CTR state transition in the codebase.

Fix: add a unit test for handle_checkin with new AES credentials that asserts the CTR
offset is reset and the new key is used for subsequent decryption.
close_reason: Added dispatch coverage for AES key rotation, CTR offset reset, and post-checkin encryption from block zero.

title: encrypt_agent_data silently returns empty Vec on invalid key or IV length
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/common/src/crypto.rs:51-52 — `encrypt_agent_data` swallows the `CryptoError` returned by `apply_agent_keystream` and returns an empty `Vec<u8>` instead. Callers in `agents.rs:522` and `dispatch.rs:448` build serialized job payloads using the return value without checking whether encryption actually succeeded.

If a stored key or IV is malformed (e.g., a mis-encoded base64 value), the agent will silently receive an empty encrypted response instead of an error being surfaced to the operator.

Fix: change the return type of `encrypt_agent_data` to `Result<Vec<u8>, CryptoError>` and update all call sites to propagate the error.
notes: Cursor agent (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor) claimed this task but ran out of credits before starting implementation. Working tree was left clean — no code changes were made. Pick up from scratch.
close_reason: Changed encrypt_agent_data to return Result and updated callers/tests to surface crypto errors

title: test: EventReceiver::recv does not test the channel-closed path
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/events.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `EventReceiver::recv` method (line 88-99) handles three outcomes: success, `RecvError::Lagged`, and `RecvError::Closed`. The `Lagged` path is tested (`lagging_subscriptions_are_dropped`), but the `Closed` path is never exercised. This path is triggered when the `EventBus` itself is dropped while a subscriber still holds a live receiver. Without a test, the behaviour — that `recv()` returns `None` and `is_closed()` becomes `true` — could silently regress if the channel is ever swapped for a different implementation.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create an `EventBus`, subscribe to it, then drop the bus. Call `receiver.recv().await` and assert it returns `None`. Also assert `receiver.is_closed()` is `true` and that a second call to `recv()` also returns `None` immediately.
close_reason: Added closed_bus_causes_recv_to_return_none test: drops the EventBus, verifies recv() returns None, is_closed() is true, and a second recv() also returns None immediately

title: test: add callback coverage for process listing and injection status handlers
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/process.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn handle_proc_ppid_spoof_callback` (line 13) — updates the agent’s spoofed parent PID and broadcasts the state change.
- `fn handle_process_list_callback` (line 38) — parses process rows into table output and structured JSON extras.
- `fn handle_inject_shellcode_callback` (line 275) — maps shellcode injection status codes into operator messages.
- `fn handle_inject_dll_callback` (line 313) — maps DLL injection status codes into operator messages.
- `fn handle_spawn_dll_callback` (line 347) — maps spawn-DLL status codes into operator messages.

**Why this matters**: these callbacks surface process-management and injection results directly from Demon payloads. Missing coverage leaves several operator commands dependent on unvalidated status decoding.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: PPID spoof updates the registry, process-list payloads broadcast both table output and structured rows, and successful inject/spawn payloads emit `Good` events.
2. Error path: malformed process rows or unknown injection status codes return `InvalidCallbackPayload`.
3. Edge case: empty process list returns `Ok(None)` without broadcasting, and missing agent state for PPID spoof still emits the response without panicking.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/dispatch/process.rs` or `teamserver/tests/` integration coverage for process callbacks
close_reason: Added 25 tests covering all five untested process dispatch handlers: ppid spoof (registry update, missing agent, truncated), process list (happy path with table+JSON, empty, truncated), inject shellcode/DLL/spawn DLL (all DemonInjectError variants, unknown status, truncated payload). 49 total tests in dispatch::process::tests, all passing.

title: architecture: teamserver control-plane TLS certificate rotates on every restart
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/main.rs:173-176 always calls `resolve_tls_identity(&subject_alt_names, None, ...)`, and common/src/tls.rs:170-178 only reuses an existing certificate when a cert/key config is supplied. Because the teamserver main path always passes `None`, the operator WebSocket/API listener gets a fresh self-signed certificate every time the process starts.

Impact: any operator client using certificate fingerprint pinning or a manually trusted self-signed cert has to re-trust the teamserver after every restart. This undermines the new client-side TLS hardening and makes stable certificate trust impossible for the control plane.

Fix: add durable control-plane TLS configuration (or persisted generated PEMs) for the teamserver itself and plumb those paths through `build_tls_config`, so restarts reuse the same certificate material instead of regenerating it.
close_reason: Added resolve_or_persist_tls_identity to common/src/tls.rs (generates+saves PEMs on first boot, reloads on subsequent boots). Added optional Cert block to TeamserverConfig for explicit cert paths. Updated build_tls_config to derive auto-persist paths from the profile path and use the new helper.

title: Payload builder appends non-Havoc DotNetNamePipe field to Demon config
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs:512 and crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs:606 now inject DotNetNamePipe into the packed Demon config blob. The Havoc reference builder ends the config immediately after the listener-specific fields (src/Havoc/teamserver/pkg/common/builder/builder.go:850-1021) and never serializes this field. Adding the extra trailing wide string changes the Demon config layout, so generated payloads no longer match Havocs wire format and can misparse on the original Demon side. The correct behavior is to keep DotNetNamePipe out of the packed config and, if needed, use it only in the teamserver-side runtime path generation like Havoc does.
close_reason: Removed DotNetNamePipe from packed Demon config to restore Havoc-compatible layout

title: Typo "impersonat" in token callback error message
description:
In crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs line 479, the error message reads "Failed to impersonat {user}" — missing the trailing "e" in "impersonate". This is displayed verbatim in the operator console.

Fix: change to "Failed to impersonate {user}".
close_reason: Typo already fixed — line 2262 of dispatch.rs correctly reads 'Failed to impersonate {user}'

title: test: sweep_dead_agents_at with mixed-liveness agents only marks stale ones dead
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_liveness.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / correctness

Every existing sweep test uses exactly one agent. No test verifies the sweep correctly handles a registry with a mix of stale and recently-active agents. A bug in `collect_stale_agents` (e.g., wrong timeout threshold comparison) could cause recently-checked-in agents to be killed alongside stale ones, but no test would catch it.

The returned `dead_agent_ids` Vec is also sorted; this sort is untested with more than one entry.

**Suggested test approach**:
Insert two agents: one with `last_call_in` well past the timeout and one with a recent `last_call_in`. Call `sweep_dead_agents_at` at a fixed `now`. Assert only the stale agent appears in the returned Vec and that the recently-active agent remains `active = true` in the registry.
close_reason: Added sweep_mixed_liveness_only_marks_stale_agents_dead test: 2 stale agents + 1 fresh, asserts only stale appear in sorted dead_agent_ids and fresh remains active

title: test: cover CommandJob action callbacks and Died no-broadcast path
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/output_dispatch.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The output dispatch integration suite only exercises the CommandJob::List branch. The action subcommands (Suspend, Resume, KillRemove) and the Died branch are untested even though they map raw callback fields into operator-facing success/error messages and, for Died, intentionally emit nothing. A regression here would change operator feedback for job control without failing any test.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add separate tests for successful and failed action payloads across the job action subcommands and one test that submits a Died payload and asserts no AgentResponse is broadcast.
close_reason: Added 7 integration tests covering CommandJob Suspend/Resume/KillRemove (success+failure) action callbacks and Died no-broadcast path

title: Build broken: 3 filesystem callback handlers called but not defined
description:
dispatch.rs calls handle_filesystem_callback, handle_command_output_callback, and handle_beacon_output_callback in two places each, but none of these functions are defined anywhere in the codebase. This causes 6 compile errors and prevents the workspace from building.

Affected locations:
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:172 (handle_filesystem_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:205 (handle_command_output_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:220 (handle_beacon_output_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:611 (handle_filesystem_callback in route_callback_by_command)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:621 (handle_command_output_callback in route_callback_by_command)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:624 (handle_beacon_output_callback in route_callback_by_command)

These must be implemented as async fns in dispatch.rs to fix the build. The filesystem callback should parse DemonCallback variants (CALLBACK_FILE for chunked download reassembly, CALLBACK_FILE_NOTFOUND, CALLBACK_OUTPUT, etc.) and broadcast results to operators. See the DemonCallback enum in common for the expected message types.
close_reason: Implemented the missing filesystem callback handlers and fixed shared download tracker reuse in builtin dispatch

title: test: add AgentRepository coverage for listener-bound persistence helpers
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- fn connect_with_options (line 125) — opens a SQLite pool from explicit sqlx connection options and runs migrations.
- fn create_with_listener (line 219) — inserts a new agent row while recording which listener accepted the session.
- fn create_with_listener_and_ctr_offset (line 228) — inserts a new agent row with listener metadata and the initial CTR block offset.
- fn update_with_listener (line 246) — updates a persisted agent row and its listener association.
- fn set_note (line 364) — persists the operator-authored note for an agent.
- fn get_persisted (line 375) — loads a persisted agent including listener name and CTR state.
- fn list_persisted (line 388) — lists persisted agents including listener name and CTR state.
- fn set_ctr_block_offset (line 397) — updates the stored AES-CTR block offset for an agent session.

**Why this matters**: these are core database write/read paths for agent registration, routing metadata, and crypto state. If any of them drift, agent reconnection, note persistence, or CTR resumption can fail silently after restart.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: create/update flows persist listener name, note, and CTR offset and get_persisted/list_persisted read them back accurately.
2. Error path: connect_with_options migrates a fresh database and surfaces invalid-path/open failures cleanly; repository updates against missing agents return the expected failure.
3. Edge case: create_with_listener_and_ctr_offset preserves nonzero CTR offsets across a reload and list_persisted returns deterministic ordering.

**Test location**: teamserver/tests/database.rs
close_reason: Added database integration coverage for listener-aware agent persistence helpers and enforced missing-agent update failures.

title: test: add direct unit coverage for AuthenticationFailure::message in auth
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/auth.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn message` (line 105) — maps `AuthenticationFailure` variants onto Havoc-compatible client-facing error text.

**Why this matters**: login failure wire messages currently rely on this match arm staying aligned with Havoc semantics. Without a direct assertion on both variants, a string regression could silently break operator login UX or compatibility while broader auth tests still pass.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: `AuthenticationFailure::InvalidCredentials.message()` returns `Authentication failed`.
2. Error path: `AuthenticationFailure::SessionCapExceeded.message()` returns `Too many active sessions; try again later`.
3. Edge case: assert `login_failure_message` continues to embed the selected message unchanged for each variant.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/auth.rs`
close_reason: Added direct InvalidCredentials.message() assertion and loop test verifying login_failure_message embeds .message() unchanged for both variants.

title: test: bool_from_i64 rejects negative values in addition to values > 1
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs
**Gap type**: edge case / boundary

`bool_from_i64` is tested for 0 → false, 1 → true, and 2 → error. But SQLite INTEGER columns can store negative values, and the out-of-range test only checks 2. A value like -1 is equally invalid and equally likely to appear in a corrupt or manually edited database. If the function also rejects -1 (which it should), a test would document and lock in that contract.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add assertions: `bool_from_i64("active", -1).is_err()`, `bool_from_i64("active", i64::MIN).is_err()`, and `bool_from_i64("active", i64::MAX).is_err()`.
close_reason: Added -1, i64::MIN, and i64::MAX assertions to bool_from_i64_rejects_out_of_range_values test

title: test: parse_working_hours missing-separator rejection path is untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`parse_working_hours` splits on `'-'` (line 1149) and returns `InvalidRequest` when the separator is absent. This branch is never tested. A caller providing a malformed string like `"0800"` or `"08:00:17:00"` should fail with a clear error, but without a test this path could be broken without notice — especially because the only test exercises the happy path `"08:00-17:00"`.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `parse_working_hours(Some("0800-1700"))` (missing colons, wrong parse), `parse_working_hours(Some("junk"))` (no separator), and assert each returns `Err(PayloadBuildError::InvalidRequest { .. })`.
close_reason: Added three tests covering the missing-separator rejection path: bare digits, junk input, and colon-only format

title: test: exercise login dialog submit and certificate-trust UI actions
description:
**File**: client/src/login.rs
**Gap type**: quality

`LoginState` is well covered, but `render_login_dialog` itself has no behavioral tests. The current suite does not verify that pressing Enter or clicking the Connect button returns `LoginAction::Submit` only when the form is valid, nor that a TLS failure with a pinned fingerprint surfaces `LoginAction::TrustCertificate` with the exact fingerprint. A UI regression here could silently break login submission or certificate pinning while the existing unit tests still pass because they only cover the backing state transitions.

**Suggested test approach**:
Use an `egui::Context` test harness to render the dialog with representative states. Simulate Enter/click submission for valid and invalid forms, assert `Waiting` while `connecting` is true, and verify that a TLS failure with `cert_fingerprint=Some(...)` produces `TrustCertificate(...)` when the trust button is clicked.
close_reason: Behavioral login dialog tests already cover submit gating, connecting wait state, and certificate trust fingerprint flow in HEAD

title: test: cover configured TLS identity resolution when PEM files are corrupt
description:
**File**: common/src/tls.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The TLS helper tests cover happy paths, missing files, and first-boot persistence, but they do not cover the case where configured certificate/key files exist and contain corrupt PEM. In particular, `resolve_tls_identity` and `resolve_or_persist_tls_identity` are not tested to prove they fail fast instead of silently falling back to generated material.

That behavior matters for operator trust and deployment safety: a regression here could hide broken TLS configuration by swapping in an unexpected self-signed identity.

**Suggested test approach**:
Write corrupt PEM data to configured cert/key paths, call both resolution helpers with `Some(HttpListenerCertConfig)`, and assert they return `TlsError::Pem` / `PersistTlsError::Tls(...)`. For the persistent path, also assert no auto-generated PEM files are written as a fallback.
close_reason: Added corrupt configured PEM coverage for resolve_tls_identity and resolve_or_persist_tls_identity, including no-fallback persistence assertions.

title: client: loot panel — filtering, credential categorization, and export
description: The loot panel shows a basic list. Add: filter by type (credentials, files, tokens), filter by agent, filter by time range. Credential categorization: NTLM hash, plaintext password, Kerberos ticket, certificate. File categorization: document, archive, binary. Export to CSV/JSON. Data comes from the REST API /api/loot endpoint.
close_reason: Added credential categorization (NTLM/Plaintext/Kerberos/Certificate), file categorization (Document/Archive/Binary), since/until time range filter, subcategory ComboBox, Export CSV/JSON buttons, and 14 new unit tests

title: test: add unit coverage for PayloadBuilderService::cache accessor
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn cache` (line 501) — returns the `PayloadCache` handle used by payload builds

**Why this matters**: callers outside the module can depend on this accessor for cache inspection or explicit maintenance. A refactor that returns the wrong handle or breaks aliasing would compile cleanly and silently invalidate cache-related workflows.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: construct `PayloadBuilderService::disabled_for_tests()` and verify repeated `cache()` calls expose the same cache handle semantics.
2. Error path: mutate the cache through the returned handle and assert subsequent service operations still observe that state.
3. Edge case: ensure the accessor is safe to call before any payload build has occurred.

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs`
close_reason: Added 3 unit tests for PayloadBuilderService::cache accessor: consistent handle, safe before build, and mutation visibility through returned handle

title: DEMON_INIT working_hours parsed as u32 then silently saturates on i32::MAX
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/demon.rs:319-320 — parse_init_agent reads working_hours with
read_u32_be (big-endian u32) and then converts via i32::try_from(working_hours).
unwrap_or(i32::MAX).  The Havoc protocol sends working_hours as a 32-bit signed
value (Go int32); any value with bit 31 set (> 2147483647) fails the try_from
conversion and is silently replaced with i32::MAX instead of preserving the
correct sign-extended bit pattern.

An agent configured with a working-hours mask whose top bit is set — e.g. a
bitmask encoding the Sunday 11 PM slot — will arrive at the teamserver with its
schedule corrupted to 0x7FFFFFFF, causing the server to misrepresent and
potentially mis-enforce the kill window.

Fix: read the four bytes and reinterpret as i32 directly via
i32::from_be_bytes(read_fixed::<4>(...)) rather than going through u32.
close_reason: Parsed DEMON_INIT working_hours as signed big-endian i32 and added regression coverage for top-bit-set masks

title: SMB E2E test codifies lowercase listener protocol labels
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs:324 asserts `message.info.protocol == Some("smb")` in the new SMB operator-agent end-to-end test. That hard-codes the known protocol-format regression already tracked in red-cell-c2-k9mp instead of validating Havoc-compatible labels (`Smb`, alongside `Http` and `Https`). Once the production bug is fixed, this test will fail and push the implementation back toward the wrong wire format. Update the test expectation to the Havoc-compatible label and keep it aligned with the protocol bug fix.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 3c647c0 — the SMB E2E test assertion was updated from lowercase "smb" to Havoc-compatible "Smb" as part of the protocol label fix

title: test: liveness sweep should keep side effects when audit persistence fails
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_liveness.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`mark_stale_agent_if_unchanged` intentionally spawns audit persistence after broadcasting the dead-agent event and pruning relay state, but the test suite only covers the successful audit-write case. There is no test proving that a database failure in that background audit task does not suppress the `AgentUpdate` event, leave SOCKS state behind, or prevent the agent from being marked dead.

This matters because the liveness sweep is resilience-critical: transient database failures should not keep stale agents alive or strand relay state in memory.

**Suggested test approach**:
Force the audit write to fail (for example by closing the database before the sweep or otherwise making the insert fail), then assert the stale agent is still marked dead, the operator event is still emitted, and relay state is still pruned.
close_reason: Added test sweep_side_effects_persist_when_audit_database_fails proving that a closed audit DB does not suppress agent death marking, event broadcast, or relay pruning

title: test: http_listener_pipeline callback from unregistered agent not tested
