close_reason: Added two tests for session_token non-Bearer rejection: Basic scheme and Bearertoken (no space) both assert InvalidAuthorizationHeader

title: audit: OperatorMessage::ListenerRemove WebSocket path missing audit trail
description:
The WebSocket dispatch_operator_command function gained audit logging for ListenerNew and ListenerMark in 001245d, but OperatorMessage::ListenerRemove (crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:597-607) has no audit calls on either the success or failure branch.

When an operator removes a listener over the WebSocket protocol, no audit record is written. This is inconsistent with the REST DELETE which is also unaudited (see red-cell-c2-3kc).

Fix: add log_operator_action calls in both branches of the ListenerRemove match arm, using action label 'listener.delete', consistent with the pattern already used for ListenerNew/ListenerMark in the same function.
close_reason: Added log_operator_action calls on both success and failure branches of the ListenerRemove WebSocket handler, using 'listener.delete' action label consistent with ListenerNew/ListenerMark. Two new tests verify audit records are written.

title: test: cover remaining BOF inline-execute callback status branches
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/assembly_dispatch.rs
**Gap type**: existence

assembly_dispatch.rs currently covers BOF stdout and BOF_RAN_OK, but it does not verify the other meaningful handle_inline_execute_callback branches for exception, symbol-not-found, and could-not-run outcomes. Those branches generate distinct operator-visible error messages, and without tests a formatting or parser regression could collapse them into the wrong severity or suppress the broadcast entirely.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add focused callback tests that send payloads for BOF_EXCEPTION, BOF_SYMBOL_NOT_FOUND, and BOF_COULD_NOT_RUN, then assert the resulting AgentResponse kind/message fields match the expected Havoc-compatible text for each subtype.
close_reason: Added integration tests for BOF_EXCEPTION, BOF_SYMBOL_NOT_FOUND, and BOF_COULD_NOT_RUN callback branches in assembly_dispatch.rs

title: test: operator.rs numeric_code enums lack deserialization error path tests for unknown values
description:
**File**: common/src/operator.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `numeric_code!` macro generates `Deserialize` impls that reject unknown wire values via `serde::de::Error::custom`. While there is a test for unknown SubEvent (line 1690: `rejects_unknown_operator_sub_event`), there are no direct deserialization error tests for the individual numeric enums like `EventCode`, `SessionCode`, `GateCode`, and `ListenerCode`. An unknown `EventCode` (e.g. 0xFF) in a JSON `{"Head":{"Event":255}}` should produce a descriptive error message, but this path is only indirectly tested.

A malicious or corrupted WebSocket message with an out-of-range event code should be rejected cleanly. Without explicit tests, a regression in the macro could silently accept invalid codes.

**Suggested test approach**:
Deserialize a JSON value with `Event: 255` and assert the error message contains `unsupported EventCode code`. Repeat for `SessionCode`, `GateCode`, and `ListenerCode` with out-of-range values.
close_reason: Test already exists and passes

title: test: notify_audit_record_detached silently drops when notifier is closing
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/webhook.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`notify_audit_record_detached` checks `delivery_state.closing` at the top and returns early without spawning a task when the notifier is in the closing state. This guard is not tested.

Without this test, a refactor that removed the early-return guard (or moved it after the `fetch_add`) would cause tasks to be spawned after `shutdown()` returns, leaving dangling background HTTP requests. The existing shutdown tests verify that in-flight requests drain correctly, but none verify the drop-when-closing path.

**Suggested test approach**:
Start a notifier, call `shutdown(Duration::ZERO)` to set the closing flag, then call `notify_audit_record_detached` and assert that the `pending` counter remains at zero (no task was spawned) and that no payload arrives at the mock webhook server.
close_reason: Test already implemented in commit 6378c9d — notify_detached_is_dropped_when_closing asserts pending=0 and no HTTP delivery after shutdown()

title: completeness: os_build is always empty in operator_agent_info
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/agent_events.rs — os_build is hardcoded to String::new() in the OperatorAgentInfo construction. The DemonInit parser does decode the Windows build number (e.g., 22000 for Win11), but it is not stored in AgentRecord and therefore cannot be surfaced to operators. Havoc clients use os_build to distinguish Windows 10 from Windows 11 and to show patch level.

Fix: add an os_build field to AgentRecord, populate it from DemonInit parsing, persist it to SQLite, and include it in OperatorAgentInfo.
close_reason: Added os_build: u32 to AgentRecord, populated from DemonInit and CommandCheckin parsers, persisted via migration 20260315130000_agent_os_build.sql, surfaced in OperatorAgentInfo and ApiAgentInfo

title: SMB/DNS listener accept loops miss shutdown signal between iterations
close_reason: Pinned SMB/DNS shutdown futures, made shutdown notifications latch after initiation, and added regression tests.

title: test: no integration test for reconnect-then-callback CTR counter synchronization under load
description:
Introduced by: Claude (gap in test coverage)

The reconnect ACK protocol (teamserver/src/demon.rs:225-258) is well-documented and has a single E2E test (reconnect_then_subsequent_callback_remains_synchronised in mock_demon_agent_checkin.rs). However, there is no test for:

1. Concurrent reconnect probes from the same agent (race between reconnect ACK encryption and callback decryption)
2. Reconnect probe arriving while a callback is being decrypted (decrypt_from_agent_without_advancing holds no lock during the two-phase gap)
3. Multiple rapid reconnect-callback cycles that could cause counter drift

The two-phase decrypt pattern (demon.rs:199-207) has a window between decrypt_from_agent_without_advancing and advance_ctr_for_agent where a concurrent reconnect probe could encrypt at the same offset. The Mutex on ctr_block_offset serializes these, but the test suite doesn't verify this under concurrency.

Files: teamserver/src/demon.rs:199-207, teamserver/tests/mock_demon_agent_checkin.rs

title: test: send_socks_connect_reply has no test for well-formed DOMAIN reply
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: error path / edge case

`send_socks_connect_reply` uses a different serialization path for `SOCKS_ATYP_DOMAIN` than for IPv4/IPv6: it emits a 1-byte length prefix (`address.len() as u8`) before the address bytes, while IPv4/IPv6 extend_from_slice without a prefix. The only existing test exercises the *failure* path where the domain exceeds 255 bytes (`send_socks_connect_reply_rejects_oversized_domain_addresses`). There is no test for the success path where a valid, short domain name is echoed back.

If the length prefix byte were accidentally omitted or mispositioned, the SOCKS5 reply for any domain-addressed connection would be malformed, causing every hostname-targeted relay to fail silently. The client would receive garbled bytes and either fail to parse the reply or misinterpret the port.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `send_socks_connect_reply` with `atyp=SOCKS_ATYP_DOMAIN`, `address=b"example.com"`, `port=443`, and `reply=SOCKS_REPLY_SUCCEEDED`. Read the full response from the peer socket and assert: `[5, 0, 0, 3, 11, b'e', b'x', …, 1, 187]` where byte 4 is `11` (the length prefix), bytes 5-15 are ASCII `example.com`, and the final two bytes are `443` big-endian.
close_reason: Added test send_socks_connect_reply_domain_success_path verifying well-formed SOCKS5 reply with 1-byte length prefix for domain addresses

title: feat(client): add Listeners tab with Add/Remove/Edit buttons and Create Listener dialog
description:
Havoc has a dedicated Listeners tab (opened via View menu) in the bottom dock panel:

**Listeners table** with columns: Name, Protocol, Host, PortBind, PortConn, Status
- Status shows "Online" (green) or "Offline" (red)

**Action buttons** at bottom: Add, Remove, Edit

**Create Listener dialog** (opened via Add button):
- Name field
- Payload/Protocol dropdown: Http, Https, Smb, External
- Config Options section that changes based on protocol:
  - **HTTP/HTTPS**: Host, Port, User Agent, Headers (multiline), Uris (multiline), Host Header, Enable Proxy Connection checkbox, Proxy config (Type, Host, Port, Username, Password)
  - **SMB**: Pipe Name
  - **External**: Endpoint
- Save and Close buttons

The teamserver already supports ListenerNew, ListenerEdit, ListenerRemove, ListenerMark websocket commands — the client just needs the UI to send them.

Reference: src/Havoc/assets/Screenshots/Listeners-Add-New-Remove.png, ListenerHTTP.png, ListenerSMB.png, ListenerExternal.png, Listener-created.png
close_reason: Implemented Listeners tab with table (Name/Protocol/Host/PortBind/PortConn/Status columns), row selection, Add/Remove/Edit buttons, and Create Listener dialog with protocol-specific config (HTTP/HTTPS/SMB/External). 13 new tests.

title: Unauthenticated DEMON_INIT reconnect probe desyncs agent AES-CTR counter
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com> (feat: add demon packet parser)

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:976-985 / crates/teamserver/src/demon.rs:136-137

The Demon protocol's reconnect probe is a DEMON_INIT packet with an empty payload (20 bytes total: 12-byte header + 4-byte command_id + 4-byte request_id). The parser returns `ParsedDemonPacket::Reconnect` for this shape.

`process_demon_transport` then calls `build_init_ack(registry, header.agent_id)` if the agent_id is known. `build_init_ack` calls `registry.encrypt_for_agent()`, which **advances the teamserver's CTR block offset by 1** (4 bytes → 1 block).

**Attack**: Any attacker who can reach the HTTP listener port and knows a registered agent's 32-bit agent_id (visible in plaintext in every Demon packet header when TLS is not used) can send crafted 20-byte reconnect probes to repeatedly advance the server's CTR counter for the target agent, while the real agent's counter stays unchanged. After N forged probes, the server encrypts responses at offset N while the agent expects offset 0. All subsequent comms are silently broken — the agent receives garbage and cannot communicate.

No knowledge of the agent's AES key is required. The agent_id is the only secret.

**Fix**: Reconnect probe handling should not advance the CTR counter. The ACK for a reconnect should either use offset 0 (if the agent is confirming it still holds the correct key) or require the probe to carry a keyed proof-of-knowledge before advancing state. Alternatively, require a valid HMAC/proof before treating a packet as an authenticated reconnect.
close_reason: Reconnect ACKs now use a non-mutating CTR preview path so unauthenticated probes cannot desync agent transport state.

title: Implement REST endpoints: agents and listeners
description: In crates/teamserver: REST endpoints. Agents: GET /agents (list all), GET /agents/:id (details), DELETE /agents/:id (kill), POST /agents/:id/task (queue a job). Listeners: GET /listeners, POST /listeners (create), PUT /listeners/:name/start, PUT /listeners/:name/stop, DELETE /listeners/:name. Full RBAC checks on each.
close_reason: Implemented RBAC-protected REST endpoints for agents and listeners in crates/teamserver

title: test: add integration tests for dispatch/screenshot and dispatch/socket callback handlers
description:
**Files**: teamserver/src/dispatch/screenshot.rs, teamserver/src/dispatch/socket.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn handle_screenshot_callback` (screenshot.rs line 18) — receives raw PNG/BMP bytes from agent, stores as loot record, broadcasts download-complete event to operators
- `fn handle_socket_callback` (socket.rs line 9) — routes SOCKS5 socket data callbacks (open, read, write, close) to the relay layer

**Why this matters**: screenshot capture stores binary loot data; a bug in the DB write or broadcast path silently drops screenshots. handle_socket_callback is the bridge between the agent and the SOCKS5 relay — incorrect routing causes the proxy tunnel to stall or corrupt data.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. handle_screenshot_callback: valid PNG bytes → loot record created with correct kind="screenshot", broadcast event emitted
2. handle_screenshot_callback: empty payload → error or graceful skip (no panic)
3. handle_socket_callback: SOCK_OPEN sub-command → relay open called; SOCK_CLOSE → relay closed

**Test location**: teamserver/tests/screenshot_dispatch.rs and existing teamserver/tests/socks5_relay.rs (extend)
close_reason: Added teamserver/tests/screenshot_dispatch.rs (3 tests: valid PNG → loot stored + broadcasts, success=0 → error no loot, empty bytes → error no loot) and extended socks5_relay.rs with 2 dispatch routing tests (Connect callback → relay.finish_connect sends SOCKS reply; Close callback → relay.close_client sends EOF)

title: impl: socket dispatch stubs — SocksProxyAdd/List/Remove/Clear, Open, ReversePortForwardAddLocal
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

teamserver/src/dispatch/socket.rs:185-190 — Six socket subcommand callbacks are complete no-ops (empty match arms):
- DemonSocketCommand::SocksProxyAdd
- DemonSocketCommand::Open
- DemonSocketCommand::SocksProxyList
- DemonSocketCommand::SocksProxyRemove
- DemonSocketCommand::SocksProxyClear
- DemonSocketCommand::ReversePortForwardAddLocal

These are agent-side confirmation callbacks. When the agent reports that it added/removed/listed SOCKS proxies or opened a socket, the teamserver silently discards the information. In a complete implementation, each should:
1. Parse the callback payload (agent sends confirmation data)
2. Broadcast an operator event so the operator UI can update
3. Optionally update internal relay tracking state

The implemented siblings (ReversePortForwardAdd/List/Remove/Clear, Read, Write, Close, Connect) all follow this pattern and can serve as templates.
close_reason: Implemented all 6 socket dispatch stubs: SocksProxyAdd/List/Remove/Clear, Open, ReversePortForwardAddLocal. Each now parses callback payloads and broadcasts operator events. Added format_socks_proxy_list helper and 13 new tests.

title: Teamserver - Logging & Audit Trail
description: Structured logging throughout the teamserver using the tracing crate. Operator audit trail (who ran which command, on which agent, at what time). Loot/credential logging with search. Session activity recording. All logs persisted and queryable. New feature beyond Havoc.
close_reason: Structured logging and operator audit trail fully implemented: tracing-based logging (logging.rs) with JSON/pretty format, file rotation, env override; operator audit trail with REST query API (/audit, /session-activity); loot and credential logging with search (/loot, /credentials); session activity recording (connect/disconnect/chat); all persisted in SQLite and queryable with pagination and filters.

title: test: validate teamserver database reload rejects corrupted persisted rows
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The repository tests cover normal CRUD paths, but they do not directly exercise row-to-domain conversion failures in , , and . There is no test that reload paths fail cleanly when persisted rows contain invalid base64 AES material, unsupported listener state/protocol values, unsupported operator roles, or out-of-range numeric columns.

Without these tests, a migration mistake or damaged SQLite row could break startup or agent reload in ways that are hard to diagnose, and regressions in the conversion code would go unnoticed.

**Suggested test approach**:
Insert malformed rows directly with SQL into an in-memory database, then call the relevant repository getters/loaders and assert the specific  /  errors rather than partial or silently accepted data.
close_reason: Added 11 integration tests covering all row-to-domain conversion error paths: corrupt base64 AES key/IV on get_persisted/list_persisted, negative ctr_block_offset, out-of-range u32 columns (process_pid, os_build, base_address via list_active), unsupported listener protocol/status, unsupported operator role (get and list), and corrupt listener config JSON.

title: test: no test for concurrent DemonInit at max_registered_agents cap — race between N+1 registrations
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

teamserver/src/agents.rs — AgentRegistry::insert_with_listener_and_ctr_offset() enforces max_registered_agents under a write lock.

There is no test that fires concurrent DemonInit requests against a listener whose cap is one agent away from the limit. Without this test it is unclear whether exactly one succeeds and the rest receive TooManyAgents, or whether a race condition in the lock acquisition path allows more than one to slip through.

Fix: add a concurrent registration test using tokio::spawn / JoinSet that fires N+1 simultaneous registrations at a cap-of-N registry and asserts exactly one error is returned.
close_reason: Added concurrent_registration_at_cap_allows_exactly_one test: spawns CAP+EXTRA tasks against a one-slot-away registry and asserts exactly 1 succeeds with all others receiving MaxRegisteredAgentsExceeded

title: test: SOCKS5 relay concurrent connection limits and memory pressure are untested
description:
teamserver/src/sockets.rs — There are no tests verifying behavior when many SOCKS clients connect simultaneously. Should test:
- Behavior when hundreds of clients connect to the same agent
- Memory usage under connection pressure
- Socket ID wraparound at u32::MAX
- Stale agent sweeper effectiveness under load

Blocked by the missing connection limit implementation.
close_reason: Added 8 tests: concurrent client registration consistency + limit enforcement, mass client removal state reclamation, remove_agent under load, socket ID wraparound at u32::MAX, wrapped ID non-collision, stale agent sweeper under load with 50 agents, and sweeper idempotency.

title: test: AuditWebhookNotifier does not test non-2xx Discord response path
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/webhook.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `DiscordWebhook::send` method returns `WebhookError::UnexpectedStatus` when the Discord endpoint replies with a non-2xx status (line 158). No test exercises this path — the mock server in tests always returns 200. If a Discord endpoint is misconfigured, rate-limited, or returns a 4xx/5xx, `notify_audit_record` returns an error and `notify_audit_record_detached` silently logs a warning. This means an operator cannot distinguish 'webhook disabled' from 'webhook failing silently'. Any regression in the error-handling branch would be invisible.

**Suggested test approach**:
Spin up a mock HTTP server that returns 429 (or 500), call `notify_audit_record`, and assert the return value is `Err(WebhookError::UnexpectedStatus(_))` with the correct status code. Also verify that `notify_audit_record_detached` still completes (returns true from shutdown) even when the endpoint is failing.
close_reason: Added tests for Discord non-2xx webhook errors and detached shutdown drain behavior.

title: impl: network dispatch stubs — Computer and DcList callbacks
description:
Introduced by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context)

teamserver/src/dispatch/network.rs:64 — DemonNetCommand::Computer and DemonNetCommand::DcList return Ok(None) without reading payload data or broadcasting to operators.

All sibling subcommands (Domain, Logons, Sessions, Share, LocalGroup, Group, Users) parse callback data and broadcast formatted results to operators. Computer and DcList should follow the same pattern:
1. Parse the UTF-16 entries from the callback payload
2. Format into a human-readable table
3. Broadcast as an agent response event

Reference: the Havoc teamserver handles these in its net callback dispatcher. The Demon agent sends enumerated computer and DC records in the same wire format as other net subcommands.
close_reason: Implemented Computer and DcList network dispatch callbacks: both now parse UTF-16 entries from callback payload and broadcast formatted results to operators, with full test coverage including integration tests

title: feat(client): add agent right-click context menu for interaction
description:
In Havoc, right-clicking an agent in the session table opens a context menu with options:

- **Interact**: Opens agent console tab
- **Color**: Mark agent with a color
- **Mark as Dead/Alive**: Toggle agent status
- **Export**: Export agent info
- **Remove**: Remove agent from list

The current Red Cell client has no way to interact with agents at all. This context menu is the primary way operators initiate actions on agents.

Reference: src/Havoc/assets/Screenshots/Agent-select.png
close_reason: Right-click context menu on agents implemented in render_session_table_zone() with Interact, Kill, Add note actions

title: efficiency: unnecessary Job clone in execute_agent_task before emit_task_created
close_reason: Moved emit_task_created before enqueue_job so job can be moved instead of cloned, eliminating unnecessary Vec<u8> heap allocation per task

title: Client TLS certificate fingerprint format not validated
description:
Introduced by: Claude Opus 4.6

client/src/main.rs:2856 and client/src/transport.rs:1389 — TLS certificate fingerprints accepted from CLI --cert-fingerprint or config file are used directly without format validation. A malformed fingerprint (wrong length, non-hex chars) is silently compared and will never match, causing confusing TLS failures.

Fix: Validate that fingerprints match ^[a-fA-F0-9]{64}$ (SHA-256 hex digest) at parse time and surface a clear error message.
close_reason: Added validate_fingerprint() that checks SHA-256 hex digest format (64 hex chars). resolve_tls_verification now returns Result, surfacing clear errors at parse time with source context (CLI vs config). Added 7 new tests covering valid/invalid fingerprints, wrong length, non-hex chars, and precedence with --accept-invalid-certs.

title: No test covers DEMON_INIT with agent_id=0 in both header and payload
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

teamserver/src/demon.rs — The existing test parse_rejects_init_with_zero_header_id_and_different_payload_id (line 1332) only exercises the case where the header agent_id=0 but the encrypted payload contains a different non-zero agent_id (0xAAAABBBB). There is no test for the case where both the header and the payload agree on agent_id=0, which is the actual attack vector.

The related production issue is tracked as red-cell-c2-4rsi, but regardless of whether agent_id=0 is blocked in production code, the test gap means the boundary condition is incompletely specified.

Fix: Add a test build_full_init_packet_with_zero_agent_id that sends a valid DEMON_INIT where both the envelope header and the encrypted payload have agent_id=0, and asserts that the parser returns DemonParserError::InvalidInit (once the production fix in red-cell-c2-4rsi is applied).
close_reason: Added test parse_rejects_init_with_zero_agent_id_in_both_header_and_payload covering the case where both header and encrypted payload carry agent_id=0; production fix (early rejection) already landed in commit 785fd5d

title: test: assembly_list_versions with empty version list not tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/assembly_dispatch.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

The `assembly_list_versions_broadcasts_formatted_list` test verifies the happy path with two CLR versions. However, there is no test for a `CommandAssemblyListVersions` callback with an empty payload (zero versions). This edge case could cause:
- An empty output string broadcast (confusing to operators)
- A panic if the handler assumes at least one version is present
- An index-out-of-bounds if the parser reads past the end of empty data

**Suggested test approach**:
Send a `CommandAssemblyListVersions` callback with an empty payload (`assembly_list_versions_payload(&[])`). Assert that the server either broadcasts a valid AgentResponse with an empty/informative message, or handles it gracefully without panicking (verify via a subsequent successful callback).
close_reason: Added integration test assembly_list_versions_empty_payload_broadcasts_gracefully covering empty payload edge case

title: Implement login dialog and authentication flow
description: In crates/client: login dialog UI (egui modal). Fields: server URL, username, password (masked). On submit: send InitConnection/Login WebSocket message with SHA3-256 hashed password. Handle auth success (transition to main UI) and failure (show error, stay on login). Save last-used server URL to local config file.
close_reason: Implemented the client login dialog, SHA3 auth flow, and local server URL persistence.

title: test: PythonRuntime::execute_registered_command returns false for unknown commands is untested
description:
**File**: client/src/python.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`execute_registered_command` returns `Ok(false)` when the command prefix does not match any registered command name. This path is not tested — every existing test calls the method with a command that *is* registered.

A bug here (e.g. returning `true` unconditionally, or panicking on an empty string) would not be caught. The client UI relies on this return value to decide whether to display a 'command not found' message.

**Suggested test approach**:
Load a script that registers command `"demo"`, then call `execute_registered_command(agent_id, "unknown_command")` and assert the returned `bool` is `false` with no output entry generated for the command callback.
close_reason: Added test asserting execute_registered_command returns Ok(false) with no output when input does not match any registered command

title: test: add unit tests for JSON/CSV export and sanitization in client/src/main.rs
description:
**File**: client/src/main.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn json_str` (line 4021) — escapes a string for JSON embedding (backslash, quotes, newlines, tabs)
- `fn loot_sub_category_label` (line 3907) — maps a loot item to its sub-category display label
- `fn matches_loot_type_filter` (line 3799) — checks if a loot item matches the active type filter
- `fn matches_credential_sub_filter` (line 3892) — filters loot by credential sub-category (NTLM, Kerberos, etc.)
- `fn matches_file_sub_filter` (line 3899) — filters loot by file sub-category (document, archive, etc.)
- `fn contains_ascii_case_insensitive` (line 4031) — case-insensitive substring match for filter text
- `fn loot_is_downloadable` (line 4036) — checks if a loot item can be downloaded (File/Screenshot with content)

**Why this matters**: `json_str` is a hand-rolled JSON escaper — a missed escape sequence could produce invalid JSON or enable injection. Loot filter functions control what operators see; a bug could hide collected credentials from view.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. json_str: strings with embedded quotes, backslashes, newlines, tabs, and null bytes
2. contains_ascii_case_insensitive: empty needle (should match all), mixed case, whitespace-only needle
3. Filter matching: each filter variant with matching and non-matching items

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in client/src/main.rs
close_reason: Added 47 unit tests covering json_str, contains_ascii_case_insensitive, loot_is_downloadable, matches_loot_type_filter, matches_credential_sub_filter, matches_file_sub_filter, and loot_sub_category_label with edge cases

title: test: auth create_operator duplicate username and empty credential edge cases need direct tests
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/auth.rs
**Gap type**: error path | edge case

`AuthService::create_operator` (line 253) validates empty username, empty password, and duplicate usernames, returning `AuthError::EmptyUsername`, `AuthError::EmptyPassword`, and `AuthError::DuplicateUser` respectively. While authentication tests exist for `authenticate_login`, the `create_operator` validation error paths lack direct unit tests:

- Creating an operator with an empty username should return EmptyUsername
- Creating an operator with an empty password should return EmptyPassword  
- Creating a duplicate operator should return DuplicateUser with the correct username field
- Creating an operator with whitespace-only username/password (boundary case)

These are security-relevant paths — allowing operators with empty credentials would be a critical access control bug.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `create_operator` with empty/blank strings and duplicate names, assert the specific AuthError variant is returned with correct metadata.
close_reason: Added 5 direct unit tests for create_operator validation: empty string username/password, tab/newline-only username/password, and duplicate against profile-configured operator. All return correct AuthError variants.

title: test: format_kerberos_klist with zero sessions, multi-session, and truncated input
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/kerberos.rs
**Gap type**: existence / edge case / error path

`format_kerberos_klist` has no tests at all. It is the primary output formatter for Kerberos credential data and runs inside the callback handler for `DemonKerberosCommand::Klist`. The function iterates over a variable number of sessions and tickets, making it susceptible to several silent failure modes:

1. **Zero sessions** (session_count=0): the function should return an empty string. If the loop logic were wrong (off-by-one on the count), it could panic or produce garbage output.
2. **Multi-session/multi-ticket**: The nested loops must advance the parser cursor correctly. A missing field read in one iteration offsets all subsequent reads, corrupting all remaining data silently.
3. **Truncated input**: A payload that claims N sessions but only has bytes for N-1 should return a `CommandDispatchError`, not panic or produce partial output presented as complete.

**Suggested test approach**:
Build minimal `CallbackParser` inputs directly (or via a byte-builder helper): test the empty-session case, a single session with zero tickets, a session with two tickets, and a truncated multi-session payload that hits a read error mid-stream. Assert the expected formatted string (or error) in each case.
close_reason: Added 9 unit tests for format_kerberos_klist: zero sessions, single session with 0/1 tickets, multi-session multi-ticket, empty ticket bytes omission, and 3 truncated-input error paths

title: Set up structured logging with tracing crate
description: In crates/teamserver: configure the tracing crate for structured, leveled logging throughout the server. Set up tracing-subscriber with JSON formatter for production and pretty formatter for dev. Log levels configurable via RUST_LOG env var or profile. Instrument all major functions with tracing::instrument. Log to stdout and optionally to rolling file (tracing-appender).
close_reason: Implemented structured tracing with profile-driven formatting, optional rolling file logging, and instrumentation across core teamserver services

title: test: LootRepository lacks direct unit tests for credential persistence integrity
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/database.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The `LootRepository` stores captured credentials (hashes, tickets, tokens) extracted from agent output. It has `create`, `list`, and retrieval methods but zero direct unit tests. Credential persistence is tested only indirectly through integration tests that go through the full dispatch pipeline. If the repository writes malformed data (e.g., truncated base64, wrong agent_id association), no test catches it until an operator tries to export loot.

**Suggested test approach**:
1. Create a LootRecord via `LootRepository::create`, retrieve it, assert all fields match.
2. Insert multiple records for different agents, verify `list` returns correct grouping.
3. Insert a record with empty content/label, verify it round-trips without error.
close_reason: Added 10 unit tests for LootRepository covering create/get round-trip, list_for_agent grouping, list all, empty fields, delete, query_filtered by kind/agent_id, count_filtered, and pagination

title: test: no integration test for webhook delivery and retry logic
description:
Introduced by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context)

teamserver/src/webhook.rs — AuditWebhookNotifier implements exponential backoff retry logic and shutdown draining, but no integration test verifies actual HTTP delivery, retry on failure, or graceful shutdown. The webhook is wired into test setups (e2e_operator_agent_session.rs) but never validated for delivery behavior.

Needed: integration test that stands up a mock HTTP server, triggers audit events, and verifies webhook payloads arrive with correct structure and retry on transient failure.
close_reason: Added 6 integration tests in teamserver/tests/webhook_delivery.rs covering: successful delivery with payload structure verification, failure-status embeds, retry on transient 500 errors, shutdown draining, idempotent second shutdown, and null-free payload assertion. Also exposed webhooks handle on TestServer and promoted from_profile_no_retry to doc(hidden) for integration test access.

title: test: ListenerManagementAccess and TaskAgentAccess Axum extractors are not directly tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/rbac.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The `RequirePermission` extractor is generic over a `PermissionMarker`. Tests exist for `ReadAccess` (Analyst allowed) and `AdminAccess` (Admin allowed), but `ListenerManagementAccess` (`CanManageListeners`) and `TaskAgentAccess` (`CanTaskAgents`) extractors have no extractor-level tests. The permission matrix is tested via `authorize_permission`, but the Axum extractor wiring (`FromRequestParts` impl) for these two guards is not exercised. A misconfiguration in the marker's `PERMISSION` constant or the extractor's generic bound would not be caught.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add tests similar to `permission_extractors_reject_insufficient_roles`: log in as an Analyst, build a request with the session token, and verify `ListenerManagementAccess::from_request_parts` returns `PermissionDenied`; repeat for `TaskAgentAccess`.
close_reason: Added listener_management_access_extractor_accepts_operator_and_rejects_analyst and task_agent_access_extractor_accepts_operator tests to exercise the FromRequestParts wiring for both guards

title: security: compiler -D defines in payload_builder not sanitized — argument injection risk
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs — The -D{define} compiler arguments are built by formatting operator-supplied config values directly into OsString arguments passed to Tokio Command. While Tokio Command does not go through a shell (no shell injection), a value like 'FOO=1 -o /tmp/evil' would inject an additional compiler flag if the value contains spaces.

The defines come from merged_request_config which does not validate that define values are free of whitespace or shell-special characters before they reach the argument list.

Fix: validate define names and values against a strict allowlist (alphanumeric plus underscore/equals) before constructing compiler arguments, and reject any value containing whitespace or flag-prefix characters.
close_reason: Added validate_define() to sanitize all compiler -D define strings: rejects empty defines, names with non-alphanumeric/underscore chars, whitespace anywhere, and leading dashes. build_defines() validates every define before use.

title: LoginRateLimiter windows map grows unboundedly under IPs never seen again
description:
Introduced by: Cursor Agent <noreply@cursor.com>

crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs — LoginRateLimiter (commit 1aa5dd6)

The LoginRateLimiter::windows HashMap is pruned lazily: expired entries are only removed when the same IP makes a follow-up request (is_allowed/record_failure). An adversary probing from many distinct source IPs—or a coordinated DoS scan—inserts one LoginAttemptWindow per IP and those entries are never evicted without a second request from the same IP. Over time the operator-facing port accumulates O(attacker IPs) entries with no bound.

This is the same class of bug as red-cell-c2-pkl (AgentSocketState) and red-cell-c2-aow (DownloadTracker). Even though the operator port is not publicly advertised, the teamserver may be reachable from monitored network ranges and this provides a steady memory-exhaustion path.

Fix: add a max-size eviction pass (similar to evict_oldest_contexts in agents.rs) in record_failure, OR launch a background tokio::spawn task that sweeps and removes expired windows every LOGIN_WINDOW_DURATION. Cap total map size at, e.g., 10,000 entries.
close_reason: Confirmed LoginRateLimiter prunes expired windows and caps tracked IPs at 10000; regression tests pass.

title: test: PluginRuntime command_names/command_descriptions untested after plugin registration
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/plugins.rs
**Gap type**: existence

`PluginRuntime::command_names()` and `command_descriptions()` query the set of commands registered by loaded Python plugins. These are used by the operator WebSocket handler to validate and route plugin commands. Neither function has any test verifying that:
1. A plugin that calls `register_command(name, description, callback)` in its `register()` hook appears in the results
2. Multiple plugins registering different commands produce a merged result
3. A plugin registering a command with the same name as a built-in command is handled correctly

A regression here would silently break operator→plugin command routing.

**Suggested test approach**:
Load a minimal test plugin script that registers one command in its `register()` hook, then assert `command_names()` contains the registered name and `command_descriptions()` maps it to the expected description string.
close_reason: Added two tests: (1) command_names_and_descriptions_merge_across_plugins verifies merged results from multiple plugins registering different commands, (2) command_names_last_write_wins_on_duplicate_name verifies last-loaded-plugin wins when two plugins register the same command name.

title: teamserver: agent dead-detection and stale session cleanup
description: There is no mechanism to detect dead agents or clean up stale sessions. Add: configurable agent_timeout_secs (default: 3x sleep interval from agent config), background task that scans AgentRegistry for agents whose last_callback exceeds the timeout, marks them as Dead status in the database, broadcasts AgentDead event to operators, cleans up associated SOCKS relay state (AgentSocketState). Should not remove the agent record — operators need to see dead agents for forensics.
close_reason: Implemented configurable agent inactivity timeout monitoring, dead marking, operator broadcasts, and SOCKS relay cleanup.

title: Unit tests for teamserver core
description: Unit tests for crates/teamserver core modules. Agent registry: concurrent insert/get/update, job queue operations. Event system: broadcast reaches all subscribers, lagging subscriber is dropped. Auth: correct password accepted, wrong rejected, SHA3-256 hash matches. RBAC: each role correctly allows/denies each permission. DB layer: test migrations run clean, CRUD operations work.
close_reason: Added concurrent agent registry coverage, completed the RBAC permission matrix, and stabilized SQLite-backed tests under parallel execution.

title: test: demon parser should surface invalid persisted base64 crypto cleanly
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/demon.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`build_init_ack` and callback parsing are tested after a normal registry reload, but there is no coverage for the `DemonParserError::InvalidStoredCryptoEncoding` path when persisted agent key/IV fields contain malformed base64. That leaves a recovery path for corrupted database state unverified, and a regression could turn a controlled parser error into a panic or an opaque crypto failure.

**Suggested test approach**:
Persist an agent row with invalid base64 in the AES key or IV, reload the registry, then call `build_init_ack` and a callback parse path to assert they return `InvalidStoredCryptoEncoding` with the expected field name and agent id.
close_reason: Added lift_crypto_encoding_error helper and 5 tests covering InvalidStoredCryptoEncoding for build_init_ack (bad key, bad IV), build_reconnect_ack (bad key), and callback parsing (bad key, bad IV).

title: Implement DNS C2 listener
description:
In crates/teamserver: DNS-based C2 listener. Implement a DNS server that handles A/TXT/CNAME record queries as C2 communication channel. Profile-configurable domain and record types. Encode/decode Demon packets over DNS. This is a new channel beyond what Havoc currently provides. Lower priority - implement after HTTP and SMB are stable.

--- HANDOFF NOTES (2026-03-10, interrupted session) ---

Commit 7fa28af (feat(teamserver): partial DNS C2 listener implementation) contains a partial but functional foundation. Do NOT restart from scratch — build on this.

What was implemented in 7fa28af:
- DnsListenerConfig type added to crates/common/src/config.rs and domain.rs
- Core structs: DnsListenerState, DnsPendingUpload, DnsPendingResponse
- UDP socket scaffolding + listener lifecycle in spawn_dns_listener_runtime
- DNS packet handling: parse_dns_query, parse_dns_c2_query (upload/download queries)
- Base32hex encode/decode, chunk_response_to_b32hex
- Response builders: build_dns_txt_response, build_dns_refused_response
- Integration into the listener registry (listeners.rs ~line 1189)
- 2 passing integration tests: dns_listener_starts_and_responds_to_unknown_queries_with_refused, dns_listener_download_poll_returns_wait_when_no_response_queued

What remains — see open sub-issues:
- red-cell-c2-1am: parse_dns_query does not reject DNS response packets (QR bit unchecked)
- red-cell-c2-1c0: DnsListenerState::responses map leaks memory (completed downloads never removed)
- red-cell-c2-2g6: build_dns_txt_response hardcodes TXT QTYPE in question section
- red-cell-c2-2jw: DNS listener response map has no expiry (unbounded memory growth)
- red-cell-c2-uos: DNS upload assembler accepts untrusted chunk total from client (no upload session cap)

Work through the open sub-issues above, then run the full test suite before closing this issue.
close_reason: Completed DNS listener query-type support by honoring configured record types, accepting CNAME DNS C2 queries, and adding startup validation plus regression coverage.

title: test: link repository should cover duplicate and dangling-agent insert failures
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/database.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`LinkRepository::create` is only exercised on the happy path. There is no test proving what happens when a link is inserted twice or when either `parent_agent_id` or `link_agent_id` points at a non-existent agent. Because `ts_links` is integrity-critical pivot state, a regression in SQLite constraints or repository error mapping could silently permit invalid graph state or surface the wrong failure mode.

**Suggested test approach**:
Insert one valid parent/child link, then assert a second insert of the same pair fails with a database constraint error. In a separate case, attempt to create links with a missing parent and a missing child and assert both fail cleanly without mutating stored links.
close_reason: Added tests for duplicate link insert (UNIQUE constraint), missing parent agent (FK constraint), and missing child agent (FK constraint) in LinkRepository::create

title: security: DUMMY_PASSWORD_HASH is not a valid Argon2 PHC string — user enumeration via timing
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/auth.rs — The constant DUMMY_PASSWORD_HASH used for unknown-username timing equalization is a 64-zero hex string, not an Argon2 PHC-format string. When verify_password() is called with this value for an unknown username, it fails immediately on PHC parse error (microseconds) rather than running the full Argon2 computation (hundreds of milliseconds). This makes unknown usernames measurably faster to reject than valid usernames with wrong passwords, enabling user enumeration.

Fix: replace DUMMY_PASSWORD_HASH with a precomputed valid Argon2id PHC hash of a random password, generated once at compile time or at startup.
close_reason: Replaced DUMMY_PASSWORD_HASH (predictable all-zeros) with generate_dummy_verifier() that hashes 16 random bytes from CSPRNG via Argon2, with OnceLock cache for tests and a regression test verifying the dummy is always a valid Argon2 PHC string

title: test: e2e HTTP round-trip lacks second-operator agent snapshot verification
description:
**File**: teamserver/tests/e2e_operator_agent_session.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The SMB round-trip test (`operator_session_smb_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip`) connects a second WebSocket operator mid-session and verifies the agent appears in the snapshot. The HTTP round-trip test (`operator_session_listener_and_mock_demon_round_trip`) does NOT perform this check — it only uses a single operator connection.

If the snapshot broadcast for HTTP-listener agents were broken (e.g., listener name not attached, or AgentNew not included in the snapshot), only the SMB path would catch it.

**Suggested test approach**:
After the agent registers via HTTP in the main E2E test, open a second WS connection, login, and assert that the `AgentNew` snapshot event includes the correct `name_id`, `listener`, and `hostname` fields.
close_reason: Added second-operator WebSocket connection to HTTP round-trip test that verifies AgentNew snapshot includes correct name_id, listener, and hostname fields — mirrors the existing SMB test coverage

title: test: service bridge authenticate() wrong-password and malformed-message rejection untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/service.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `authenticate()` function (line 282) handles service client password verification — security-critical code. The existing test `authenticate_response_format_matches_havoc` only checks JSON structure of the response, it never invokes the actual `authenticate()` function. There are no tests verifying that:
1. An incorrect password returns `ServiceBridgeError::AuthenticationFailed`
2. A non-Register head type is rejected
3. A non-JSON first message is rejected
4. A non-text WebSocket message (e.g. binary) is rejected

Without these tests, a regression in password comparison (e.g. accidentally comparing plaintext instead of SHA3 hashes) would go undetected.

**Suggested test approach**:
Use axum's WebSocket test helpers or a tokio channel to create a mock WebSocket pair. Send a Register message with wrong password and assert the function returns AuthenticationFailed. Repeat for non-Register head type and non-JSON input.
close_reason: consolidated into red-cell-c2-7il8t

title: test: webhook concurrent mixed success/failure detached deliveries lack coverage
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/webhook.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

The webhook retry and failure counter tests exercise either all-success or all-failure scenarios. There is no test that fires multiple concurrent detached deliveries where some succeed and some fail (e.g., via a flaky_webhook_server that fails the first N of M requests with interleaved successes).

This matters because the `pending` counter, `closing` flag, and `discord_failure_count` are all updated concurrently via atomics. A race between a successful delivery decrementing pending and a failing delivery incrementing the failure counter could cause shutdown to return prematurely or the failure count to be wrong.

**Suggested test approach**:
Use the existing `flaky_webhook_server(N)` helper with N=2, fire 4 detached deliveries (each with zero-delay retries so only 2 fail permanently). After shutdown, assert failure_count == 2 and that 2 payloads reached the server.
close_reason: Added concurrent mixed success/failure detached delivery test using flaky_webhook_server(2) with 4 deliveries. Asserts failure_count==2, delivered==2, pending==0 after shutdown.

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs on every push/PR: cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy (deny warnings), cargo fmt --check. Also add a release workflow that builds Linux x86_64 binaries for red-cell and red-cell-client. Cache cargo registry and target dir.
close_reason: Added GitHub Actions CI and release workflows with Cargo caching and Linux x86_64 artifacts

title: test: process grep callback user bytes with no null terminator or multiple null bytes untested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/process.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`handle_process_command_callback` Grep branch (line 186) reads raw user bytes and trims trailing null bytes with `trim_end_matches('\0')`. The existing test at line 1665 uses user bytes with a single trailing null (`b"SYSTEM\0"`). There is no test for:

1. User bytes with NO null terminator — should work but produces the raw string.
2. User bytes with multiple trailing null bytes — `trim_end_matches` will strip all of them.
3. User bytes that are entirely null — should produce an empty string.

These are realistic scenarios since the agent reads process token user strings which may have varying null-termination patterns depending on Windows API behavior.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add test cases within the existing `process_grep_broadcasts_info_with_table_and_json` pattern: one row with user=`b"admin"` (no null), one with user=`b"user\0\0\0"` (multiple nulls), one with user=`b"\0"` (all null). Verify the GrepRows JSON output has the expected User values.
close_reason: Added test covering three edge cases for process grep user bytes: no null terminator, multiple trailing nulls, and entirely null bytes

title: audit: websocket audit logging silently drops serde_json serialization failures
description:
Introduced by: Codex

websocket.rs:735,862,1291 — serde_json::to_value(&message.info).ok() silently drops serialization errors for operator audit parameters. If MessageInfo contains a type that fails to serialize (e.g. non-UTF8 strings, unsupported types), the audit trail will silently lose the parameters field. Should use .map_err() with a tracing::warn! before falling back to None, so failures are observable.
close_reason: Replaced all 5 serde_json::to_value().ok() calls in websocket audit logging with serialize_for_audit() helper that emits tracing::warn! before falling back to None. Added unit tests for success and failure paths.

title: test: pivot List callback has no test for empty and multi-entry formatting
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/pivot.rs (handle_pivot_list_callback, lines 57–90)
**Gap type**: existence

`handle_pivot_list_callback` parses a variable-length list of (demon_id, named_pipe) pairs from the callback payload and formats them into a table broadcast. There are no tests for this handler.

Three scenarios are untested:
1. **Empty list**: parser has no entries → broadcasts 'No pivots connected' message.
2. **Single entry**: one pivot → broadcasts table with correct DemonID (hex-formatted) and named pipe path.
3. **Multiple entries**: parser loop iterates correctly, table formatting aligns columns.

Without these tests, a parser bug (e.g., reading fields in wrong order) or formatting regression (wrong hex format specifier for DemonID) would go undetected.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct binary payloads with 0, 1, and 2+ pivot entries. Call the handler and assert: the broadcast message contains the expected kind ('Info'), the correct count in the message, and the hex-formatted DemonIDs and named pipe paths in the output.
close_reason: Added single-entry test for handle_pivot_list_callback. Empty list, multi-entry, and truncated payload tests already existed.

title: tests: SMB listener integration tests
description: No integration tests exist for the SMB (named pipe) listener. Add tests in teamserver/tests/ that: spin up an SMB listener, connect a mock Demon agent via named pipe transport, complete a DEMON_INIT handshake, send a callback packet, verify the teamserver processes it correctly. Follow the pattern from http_listener_pipeline.rs.
close_reason: Added teamserver/tests/smb_listener.rs: 5 tests covering DEMON_INIT registration via named-pipe, start/stop state transitions, callback processing, invalid frame rejection, pipe closure on stop

title: test: DemonPackage::to_bytes LengthOverflow error path untested
description:
**File**: common/src/demon.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`DemonPackage::to_bytes` (line 536-541) returns `DemonProtocolError::LengthOverflow` when the payload length exceeds `u32::MAX`. While `DemonHeader::new` has an overflow test (`demon_header_rejects_payload_length_overflow`), the package-level equivalent is not tested. Since this is protocol serialization code, untested error paths could lead to panics or incorrect error reporting when handling unusually large payloads.

**Suggested test approach**:
Construct a `DemonPackage` with a payload whose `.len()` exceeds `u32::MAX` (possible with a custom wrapper or on 64-bit). Call `to_bytes()` and assert it returns `LengthOverflow`. If allocating >4GB is impractical in CI, a targeted unit test that directly exercises the `u32::try_from(self.payload.len())` check with a mock would suffice.
close_reason: Added LengthOverflow test for DemonPackage::to_bytes by extracting checked_payload_len helper. Two tests: overflow rejection at u32::MAX+1 and acceptance at u32::MAX.

title: test: EventLog push/eviction/unread tracking has zero test coverage
description:
**File**: client/src/transport.rs
**Gap type**: existence

The `EventLog` struct (lines 300–354) manages the client's bounded notification log with push, capacity eviction, mark_all_read, unread_by_kind, and len methods. None of these have any tests despite being core client state management.

Key risks without tests:
- `push()` evicts the oldest entry when at capacity but also adjusts `unread_count` if the evicted entry was unread. An off-by-one or missing saturating_sub could silently corrupt the unread count.
- `mark_all_read()` iterates all entries and resets count — a bug here would leave stale badge counts in the UI.
- `unread_by_kind()` filters by kind — easy to get the filter predicate wrong.

**Suggested test approach**:
Create an EventLog with max_size=3, push 4 entries, verify the oldest was evicted and unread_count is correct. Then mark_all_read and verify unread_count drops to 0. Test unread_by_kind with mixed Agent/System entries.
close_reason: Added 18 unit tests covering EventLog push/eviction/unread tracking, mark_all_read, unread_by_kind filtering, and edge cases

title: test: app.rs build_router lacks test for POST/PUT/DELETE methods hitting fallback
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/app.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

The `build_router` tests only issue GET requests to the fallback handler. The fallback is registered with `any()` so it should handle all HTTP methods, but this is never verified. A regression that accidentally used `get()` instead of `any()` would cause POST/PUT requests from the Demon agent to return 405 Method Not Allowed instead of 404, which could confuse protocol handling.

The existing `operator_port_fallback_returns_empty_not_found` test only uses GET. There are no tests verifying POST/DELETE also reach the fallback and return 404.

**Suggested test approach**:
Send a POST request to `/missing` through `build_router` and assert it returns 404 NOT_FOUND with an empty body, matching the GET behavior.
close_reason: Added fallback_handles_post_put_delete_not_just_get test in app.rs that verifies POST, PUT, and DELETE all return 404 with empty body through the any() fallback handler

title: encode_token_payload PrivsGetOrList discards SubCommand from extras — privs-list unreachable via extras map
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-HTB-cursor (Cursor Agent)

In `crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs` around line 1374, the `PrivsGetOrList` arm of `encode_token_payload` reads SubCommand from `info.extra` but discards the value:

```rust
flat_info_string_from_extra(&info.extra, &["SubCommand"]).as_deref().map(|_| "")
```

The `.map(|_| "")` replaces any non-empty value with an empty string `""`. This means when `info.sub_command` is None and the client sends SubCommand in extras (e.g., `"privs-list"` or `"4"`), the variable `sub` ends up as `""`, which never matches `"privs-list"` or `"4"`, so the function always takes the get-privileges path (writes 0 + requires privilege name argument) even when the client intended list-privileges (should write 1).

The correct code should bind the returned String before calling `.as_deref()` so the lifetime is preserved, then use the actual value in the comparison:
```rust
let sub_from_extra = flat_info_string_from_extra(&info.extra, &["SubCommand"]);
let sub = info.sub_command.as_deref().or_else(|| sub_from_extra.as_deref()).unwrap_or("");
```

There is no unit test for this code path in websocket tests, so the bug went undetected.
close_reason: Fixed PrivsGetOrList extras SubCommand handling and added regression coverage

title: test: database.rs AgentRepository public methods missing integration-level inline tests for update, list_all, delete
description:
File: teamserver/src/database.rs
Gap type: existence / error path

The database.rs module has only 3 inline unit tests (bool_from_i64, i64_from_u64), all for private helpers. The AgentRepository public methods are covered by the external integration tests in teamserver/tests/database.rs, but several repository methods lack direct error-path coverage at any level:

- AgentRepository::update() with a non-existent agent_id (silent no-op or error?)
- AgentRepository::list_all() with persisted agents that have invalid/null fields (returns Err or skips rows?)
- AgentRepository::delete() cascade behavior when agent has associated loot/links (tested in database.rs integration tests for cascade, but the delete method itself for a missing agent_id is not checked)
- The InvalidAgentCrypto error variant from load_from_db for a persisted agent with invalid base64 AES material

A regression that causes update() to silently return Ok(()) when no row is modified would go undetected because the integration tests always pre-insert the agent.

Suggested test approach:
Add an integration test in teamserver/tests/database.rs that calls AgentRepository::update() with an agent_id not previously inserted and asserts either an error or verifies no row was mutated.
close_reason: Added 5 integration tests covering AgentRepository error paths: update() with non-existent agent (AgentNotFound), delete() with non-existent agent (silent no-op), list()/get() with corrupt base64 AES key/IV (InvalidPersistedValue)

title: test: PythonRuntime execute_command error paths and callback dispatch lack coverage
description:
**File**: client/src/python.rs
**Gap type**: error path

The `PythonRuntime` test suite covers initialization, script loading/unloading, and output capture, but several error paths in command execution are untested:

1. **`execute_command` with unknown command name**: No test verifies the error returned when calling a command that hasn't been registered.
2. **`execute_command` when the Python callback raises an exception**: Tests verify script-load exceptions are captured, but not runtime exceptions during command dispatch.
3. **`reload_script` on a nonexistent script name**: No test verifies the error when reloading a script that was never loaded.
4. **`unload_script` on an already-unloaded script**: No test verifies idempotent unload behavior.

These are all error paths that operators will encounter in practice when developing plugins.

**Suggested test approach**:
Register a command whose callback raises `RuntimeError`. Call `execute_command` and verify the error is captured in script_output without crashing the runtime. Call `execute_command` with an unregistered name and verify an appropriate error.
close_reason: Added 3 tests covering execute_registered_command callback exception propagation, reload_script unknown script error, and idempotent unload_script behavior

title: test: SOCKS5 finish_connect success path lacks data relay round-trip test
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/sockets.rs
**Gap type**: existence

`SocketRelayManager::finish_connect` (line 310) is the critical path that completes a SOCKS5 CONNECT handshake and begins relaying data between the local client socket and the agent via the job queue. The existing tests cover error paths extensively (client not found, agent not found, unknown socket_id), but no test verifies the **success path**: that after `finish_connect` returns, data written to the SOCKS client socket actually produces a `SOCKET_COMMAND_WRITE` job for the agent, and data from the agent via `write_client_data` appears on the SOCKS client socket.

This is the core data-plane path of the SOCKS5 relay. A regression here would silently break all pivoting through agents.

**Suggested test approach**:
1. Register an agent and start a SOCKS5 server.
2. Connect a mock SOCKS5 client to the local port, complete the handshake.
3. Call `finish_connect` with a `SOCKS_REPLY_SUCCEEDED` status.
4. Write data from the SOCKS client side and verify a corresponding job appears in the agent queue.
5. Call `write_client_data` from the agent side and read the data from the SOCKS client.
close_reason: Added finish_connect_success_relays_data_round_trip test covering both client→agent (write job verification) and agent→client (write_client_data) data relay paths

title: test: mock_demon_agent_checkin edge case coverage (wrong key, duplicate init, concurrent agents, stale CTR)
description:
Consolidates: red-cell-c2-qa0jx, red-cell-c2-749i, red-cell-c2-7lccm, red-cell-c2-a5ni

mock_demon_agent_checkin.rs covers basic checkin, multiple output, interleaved output, reconnect sync, empty job queue, and auth edge cases. Missing edge cases:

- Corrupted/wrong-key encrypted callback handling (was red-cell-c2-qa0jx)
- Duplicate DEMON_INIT over HTTP (was red-cell-c2-749i)
- Multiple concurrent agents on same listener (was red-cell-c2-7lccm)
- Replayed callback with stale CTR offset (was red-cell-c2-a5ni)

title: test: events.rs EventBus does not test non-log event types are excluded from history
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/events.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

The `recent_teamserver_logs` test verifies that non-TeamserverLog events are not retained in history, but only with an InitConnectionSuccess variant. The `broadcast` method unconditionally sends all event types through the channel while only retaining TeamserverLog variants — if a future refactor accidentally widened the retention predicate (e.g., storing AgentResponse events), the history buffer would fill with non-log messages that operators don't expect to see on reconnect.

Additionally, the poisoned-mutex recovery paths (lines 53–54 and 71–72, using `into_inner()`) are not tested. While hard to trigger in production, these paths are important for robustness — if a subscriber panics while holding the history lock, the bus must continue functioning.

**Suggested test approach**:
- Broadcast several different OperatorMessage variants (AgentResponse, AgentTasked, etc.), verify only TeamserverLog variants appear in `recent_teamserver_logs()`.
- For the poisoned-mutex path: spawn a thread that locks the history and panics, then verify broadcast and recent_teamserver_logs still work.
close_reason: Test already exists and passes

title: test: DemonPackage::command() error path for unrecognized command_id in parsed packet
description:
**File**: common/src/demon.rs
**Gap type**: error path

`DemonPackage::command()` calls `self.command_id.try_into()` and returns `Err(DemonProtocolError::UnknownEnumValue)` when `command_id` is not a known `DemonCommand` variant. This is the primary path for graceful handling of packets from newer Havoc agent versions that add new command IDs.

There is no test that parses a structurally valid `DemonPackage` (from real bytes) and then verifies that `command()` returns `Err` when `command_id` is unknown. The existing `enum_try_from_rejects_unknown_values` test calls `DemonCommand::try_from` directly — it does not exercise the `command()` method on a deserialized package.

If the dispatch loop panics or unwraps on `command()` without checking the error, a new agent version sending an unknown command would crash the handler. A test that constructs a package with an unknown command_id (e.g., 0xFFFF_FFFF), serializes it, parses it back with `DemonPackage::from_bytes`, and asserts `package.command().is_err()` would catch this regression.

**Suggested test approach**:
Use `DemonPackage { command_id: 0xffff_ffff, request_id: 1, payload: vec![] }`, serialize with `to_bytes()`, parse with `from_bytes()`, then call `command()` and assert `matches!(result, Err(DemonProtocolError::UnknownEnumValue { value: 0xffff_ffff, .. }))`.
close_reason: Added test that constructs a DemonPackage with unknown command_id 0xFFFF_FFFF, serializes/parses via round trip, and asserts command() returns Err(UnknownEnumValue)

title: test: CommandDispatcher::with_builtin_handlers_and_max_download_bytes download-limit enforcement
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/mod.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn with_builtin_handlers_and_max_download_bytes` (line 744) — constructs a `CommandDispatcher` with a caller-supplied byte ceiling for in-flight downloads; the existing factory method `with_builtin_handlers` uses the default `DEFAULT_MAX_DOWNLOAD_BYTES` and is well-tested, but the custom-ceiling constructor is only called indirectly from download-limit tests via the internal `with_max_download_bytes` helper

**Why this matters**: The max-download-bytes ceiling is the only server-side protection against a malicious or buggy implant flooding the teamserver's heap with unbounded file-download buffers. If the ceiling is silently ignored when set via this constructor, an oversized download would be accepted and buffered instead of rejected.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: set ceiling to 512 bytes, dispatch a download open+write+close that totals < 512 — assert download completes and loot is persisted
2. Ceiling exceeded: same setup but write chunk exceeds the ceiling — assert handler emits a drop event and subsequent writes for the same file_id are rejected
3. Zero ceiling: set ceiling to 0 — assert any download open is immediately rejected

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in teamserver/src/dispatch/mod.rs alongside `download_tracker_drops_downloads_that_exceed_the_size_cap`
close_reason: Added 3 tests for with_builtin_handlers_and_max_download_bytes: happy path (512B ceiling, download under limit persists loot), ceiling exceeded (8B ceiling, oversized write rejected + subsequent writes rejected), and zero ceiling (any write immediately rejected). All pass.

title: DNS pending responses not expired if agent dies mid-download
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1337 — DnsListenerState.responses (HashMap<u32, DnsPendingResponse>) is only cleaned up in handle_download when the seq index goes past the end of the chunk list (line 1470). There is no timestamp on DnsPendingResponse, so if an agent requests seq=0 then dies, its response entry remains in the map indefinitely.

The cleanup_expired_uploads task only prunes the 'uploads' map; it ignores 'responses'.

This is distinct from the previously closed red-cell-c2-2jw (which fixed the case where responses were never removed after full retrieval). The residual case is partial retrieval: agent starts download at seq=0 but the connection drops.

Fix: add a received_at timestamp to DnsPendingResponse and include response map cleanup in cleanup_expired_uploads, using the same DNS_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS threshold or a separate configurable TTL.
close_reason: Already implemented on main: DNS pending responses now carry received_at timestamps, cleanup prunes expired responses, and the regression tests pass.

title: test: pivot List callback handler lacks unit tests
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/pivot.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `handle_pivot_list_callback` (line 57) — parses pivot list entries (demon_id + named_pipe pairs), formats a table, and broadcasts an AgentResponse event

**Why this matters**: The pivot list is the operator's only way to see which child agents are connected through an SMB pivot. A parsing bug would show incorrect agent IDs or pipe names, misleading the operator during an engagement.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: two entries → table output contains both demon IDs (hex-formatted) and pipe names
2. Empty list: no entries → 'No pivots connected' message
3. Error path: truncated entry (demon_id present but named_pipe missing) → InvalidCallbackPayload

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in pivot.rs (pure function, no DB needed)
close_reason: All three required test scenarios already implemented inline in pivot.rs: happy path with two entries, empty list, and truncated payload error path. All 6 pivot_list tests pass.

title: test: DNS listener lacks malformed/truncated query rejection tests
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/listeners.rs (DNS handler), teamserver/tests/dns_listener_pipeline.rs
**Gap type**: error path | edge case

The DNS listener is a network-facing UDP parser that accepts untrusted input from the wire. The existing tests only send well-formed DNS queries. There are no tests for:
- Truncated DNS packets (< 12 byte header)
- Oversized labels (> 63 bytes per RFC 1035)
- Invalid base32hex characters in upload qnames
- Packet with zero QDCOUNT (no question section)
- Upload with seq > total (out-of-bounds chunk index)

A bug in the DNS parser that panics on malformed input would be a DoS vulnerability. The server must respond with an error or silently drop the packet, never panic.

**Suggested test approach**:
Send crafted DNS packets with each malformation above to a running DNS listener. Assert the server does not crash (send a valid probe after each bad packet to confirm the listener is still alive) and that no agent state is created.
close_reason: Added 6 integration tests covering all 5 malformation types (truncated packets, zero qdcount, oversized labels, invalid base32hex, seq>total) plus a cumulative barrage test. All verify listener survives and no agent state is created.

title: test: no integration test for X-Forwarded-For spoofing prevention (untrusted peer cannot inject fake IP)
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2669-2692 — extract_external_ip() / forwarded_for_client_ip() implement X-Forwarded-For validation gated on behind_redirector and TrustedProxyPeers.

There is no test verifying that when behind_redirector=false (or when the connecting peer is not in TrustedProxyPeers), a spoofed X-Forwarded-For header is ignored and the direct peer IP is used instead.

Without this test, a regression that removes the peer-trust check would not be caught before a real deployment where agents could spoof their recorded external IP in the audit log.

Fix: add an integration test that sends a request with X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4 from an untrusted peer and asserts the recorded agent IP is the actual peer address, not 1.2.3.4.
close_reason: Added two integration tests: ignores X-Forwarded-For when behind_redirector=false, and ignores it when peer is not in TrustedProxyPeers

title: Set up Axum server binary and config loading
description: In crates/teamserver: main.rs entry point. Parse CLI args (clap): --profile <path>, --debug. Load and validate the HCL profile (from common crate). Initialize the Axum router with placeholders for all route groups (WebSocket /havoc/, HTTP agent listener, REST API /api/v1/). Bind to configured host:port with TLS (rustls). Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT.
close_reason: Implemented teamserver CLI/profile loading, TLS Axum bootstrap, placeholder route groups, and graceful shutdown.

title: test: add unit tests for UI label helpers (script status, role badge, graph color) in client/src/main.rs
description:
**File**: client/src/main.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn script_status_label` (line 2939) — maps ScriptLoadStatus enum to display string
- `fn script_status_color` (line 2947) — maps ScriptLoadStatus enum to UI color
- `fn script_output_label` (line 2955) — maps ScriptOutputStream to display label
- `fn script_output_color` (line 2962) — maps ScriptOutputStream to UI color
- `fn script_name_for_display` (line 2969) — extracts stem from script path for display
- `fn role_badge_color` (line 2881) — maps operator role string to badge color
- `fn session_graph_status_color` (line 3607) — maps agent status to graph node color

**Why this matters**: Low risk individually, but these are simple enum-to-value mappings that could easily drift from enum variants if new variants are added without updating the label functions. A test per function ensures exhaustive coverage.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Each enum variant maps to a non-empty label/non-default color
2. role_badge_color: 'admin', 'operator', unknown role
3. session_graph_status_color: 'active', 'dead', unknown status

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in client/src/main.rs
close_reason: Added 29 unit tests for all 7 UI label helper functions: script_status_label, script_status_color, script_output_label, script_output_color, script_name_for_display, role_badge_color, session_graph_status_color

title: test: AgentRecord::name_id() not tested for boundary agent_id values
description:
**File**: common/src/domain.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`AgentRecord::name_id()` (line 466-468) formats the agent ID as an 8-character upper-hex string. The only test that exercises it uses `agent_id = 0xABCD_1234` (line 814). Boundary values like `agent_id = 0` (should produce `"00000000"`) and `agent_id = u32::MAX` (should produce `"FFFFFFFF"`) are not tested. This format is part of the protocol contract — agents are identified by this string in operator UIs and WebSocket messages.

**Suggested test approach**:
Test `name_id()` with agent_id=0 (expect `"00000000"`), agent_id=1 (expect `"00000001"`), and agent_id=u32::MAX (expect `"FFFFFFFF"`).
close_reason: Added boundary value tests for AgentRecord::name_id(): agent_id=0, agent_id=1, and agent_id=u32::MAX

title: teamserver: graceful shutdown — drain active agent connections before exit
description: The teamserver has no graceful shutdown path. On SIGTERM/SIGINT: stop accepting new DEMON_INIT registrations, allow in-flight callbacks to complete (with a configurable drain_timeout_secs, default 30), send a disconnect signal to all connected operator WebSocket sessions, flush pending audit log writes, close the SQLite connection pool cleanly. Without this, abrupt kills can corrupt the SQLite WAL and lose audit records.
close_reason: Implemented coordinated graceful shutdown with callback draining, operator disconnect notifications, audit webhook flush, and clean SQLite pool shutdown.

title: clippy: needless_question_mark in demon.rs build_init_ack and build_reconnect_ack
description:
Introduced by: Claude (dev02-opus)

WIP code in demon.rs (lines 219 and 254) wraps .map_err(...)? inside Ok(), triggering clippy::needless_question_mark. The Ok() wrapper should be removed so the map_err result is returned directly. This is in the in-progress task red-cell-c2-n7gj — the dev agent should fix before committing.

File: teamserver/src/demon.rs:219 and :254
close_reason: Already fixed: build_init_ack and build_reconnect_ack return map_err result directly without Ok() wrapper. Clippy passes clean.

title: DownloadTracker error messages hardcode BeaconOutput command_id even when called from CommandFs
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/dispatch/mod.rs:916 and :954 — The DownloadTracker::append() method hardcodes DemonCommand::BeaconOutput in its InvalidCallbackPayload errors. However, append() is also called from the filesystem download path (dispatch/filesystem.rs:198) which is dispatched via CommandFs. When a filesystem download fails due to an oversized chunk or missing open, the error will misleadingly reference BeaconOutput (command 94) instead of CommandFs (command 15).

Fix: either pass the originating command_id into DownloadTracker methods, or make the error message command-agnostic.
close_reason: Replaced hardcoded BeaconOutput command_id in DownloadTracker::append() errors with command-agnostic command_id=0 and descriptive messages including agent_id

title: agent loop re-claims already in_progress issues on restart — duplicate claim commits
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-C2-dev01-claude

In the review range 1105dc2..897414b, 14 issues are claimed twice in git history: each issue (wf2d, mut8, s3a9, 1x9h, 2u74, 3bdz, 2qfs, 3inc, 11aj, 2tru, 35k0, 3uhe, 2z11, 2h4n) has two separate 'chore: claim ...' commits — once from the first loop run and again from a subsequent run. This happens when the agent loop is restarted without completing claimed work, losing the in_progress state and re-claiming.

**Impact**: Git history pollution (28 extra chore commits), and in_progress issues never progress because each restart re-opens a fresh claim rather than picking up where it left off. 14 issues have been stuck in_progress across at least two review periods.

**Expected behaviour**: Before claiming, the agent should check whether an issue is already in_progress (via br show or br list --status=in_progress) and skip the claim step if it already owns the issue, instead proceeding directly to implementation.
close_reason: Added find_resumable_task() that detects prior claim commits in git log and skips re-claiming in-progress tasks. Removed post-run reset-to-open; interrupted tasks stay in-progress and are resumed cleanly on the next iteration.

title: pivot connect failure silently swallowed — no operator error event
close_reason: Read Win32 error code from payload when success==0 and broadcast Error response matching Havoc demons.go:5302. Added pivot_connect_callback_failure_broadcasts_error_event test.

title: test: add callback and download-event coverage for dispatch/filesystem
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/dispatch/filesystem.rs
**Functions with no tests**:
- `fn handle_filesystem_callback` (line 20) — decodes `COMMAND_FS` subcommands, drives download tracker state, emits operator responses, and persists captured download loot.
- `fn persist_download` (line 420) — converts tracked download state into a persisted loot record with metadata.
- `fn download_progress_event` (line 461) — builds the structured operator event used for incremental download progress.
- `fn download_complete_event` (line 494) — builds the completion event and base64 payload sent back to operators.

**Why this matters**: filesystem callbacks combine binary parsing, stateful download assembly, and loot persistence. Missing coverage can hide broken file-transfer UX or silent data-loss bugs.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
