- `pub(super) async fn handle_transfer_callback` (line 20) — dispatches transfer subcommands (List, Upload, Download) by parsing a binary callback payload; formats a file-transfer status table when subcommand is List
- `pub(super) async fn handle_mem_file_callback` (line 145) — parses a mem-file callback, emits an operator event with the captured bytes, and persists loot
- `pub(super) async fn handle_package_dropped_callback` (line 169) — parses a package-dropped callback and broadcasts a structured operator event
- `pub(super) async fn handle_beacon_output_callback` (line 191) — parses free-form beacon output, persists credentials if present, and emits output and loot events

**Why this matters**: These handlers sit directly in the agent → teamserver callback path. A parse error or off-by-one in the binary payload reader would silently drop or corrupt transfer-related agent responses — the kind of bug that only surfaces during a live operation.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. `handle_transfer_callback` List subcommand — craft a binary payload with one active download entry; assert the returned `None` (no reply) and that the formatted output contains the file name and progress percentage
2. `handle_transfer_callback` with truncated payload — assert `CommandDispatchError::InvalidCallbackPayload` is returned
3. `handle_mem_file_callback` — supply a valid mem-file payload; assert the operator event is broadcast and loot is stored
4. `handle_beacon_output_callback` — supply output containing a credential marker; assert credential is persisted and the output event is emitted

**Test location**: inline `#[cfg(test)]` in `teamserver/src/dispatch/transfer.rs` using the same mock-agent helpers used by `dispatch/filesystem.rs` tests
close_reason: added five integration tests covering handle_transfer_callback (List subcommand + truncated), handle_mem_file_callback, handle_package_dropped_callback, and handle_beacon_output_callback with credential persistence

title: security: TLS private key stored without Zeroizing wrapper in common/src/tls.rs
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

common/src/tls.rs — TlsIdentity struct stores the rustls PrivateKey as a plain Vec<u8> without a Zeroizing<> wrapper.

AgentEncryptionInfo in common/src/domain.rs correctly wraps AES keys with Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>, establishing a zeroization policy for key material. The TLS private key is exempt from this policy despite being equally sensitive.

On a long-running process with TLS cert reloads, the old private key bytes may persist in freed heap memory until overwritten. Consistent application of the key-material policy requires wrapping the private key field in Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>.

Fix: change the private key storage type to Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> in TlsIdentity (or whichever struct holds the decoded key bytes in common/src/tls.rs).
close_reason: Wrapped TlsIdentity::private_key_pem in Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> to apply the same key-material zeroization policy used by AgentEncryptionInfo.

title: dev01-claude stuck in repeated claim/interrupt loop on 15+ test tasks
description:
Introduced by: Ubuntu-C2-dev01-claude

In the review period 73cb663..a73963a, Ubuntu-C2-dev01-claude is repeatedly claiming and immediately interrupting the same test tasks with zero code output. Each task is being claimed-then-interrupted at least twice (some three times) with ~5-minute cycles:

Affected issues (all in_progress, blocking other agents):
red-cell-c2-8gri, red-cell-c2-upwv, red-cell-c2-0eb7, red-cell-c2-y4fs, red-cell-c2-yxty, red-cell-c2-xypk, red-cell-c2-5b4v, red-cell-c2-doop, red-cell-c2-rqen, red-cell-c2-3b4x, red-cell-c2-ldu0, red-cell-c2-enwz, red-cell-c2-vidy, red-cell-c2-zpn2, red-cell-c2-uyk8, red-cell-c2-m47p, red-cell-c2-t4fy

Evidence: e.g. red-cell-c2-8gri was claimed at c1fc789, interrupted at 0875bcd, reclaimed at 877683e, interrupted again at a73963a — all with only log/beads file changes and no test code.

**Impact**: These 17 issues are stuck in_progress and not visible in `br ready`, blocking other agents from picking them up. Dev01-claude is consuming session slots without producing output.

**Fix**: The dev01 agent should reset these issues to open status so other agents can claim them, or investigate what is causing the repeated context-limit interruptions.
close_reason: All 17 stuck issues have been completed and closed by dev01-claude with actual test implementations. The claim/interrupt loop resolved itself — no further action needed.

title: Teamserver - Core Infrastructure
description: Core teamserver infrastructure: Axum HTTP server setup, SQLite database layer (sqlx), in-memory agent registry, and event broadcast system. This is the backbone that all other teamserver components build upon.
close_reason: Core infrastructure verified complete: Axum server setup, SQLite/sqlx persistence, agent registry, and event bus are implemented and passing workspace quality gates.

title: Switch pyo3 to abi3 stable ABI (remove Python version pin)
description:
Switch both the teamserver and client from a version-pinned pyo3 build to the stable ABI (abi3) so the binaries run against any Python 3.x >= the chosen minimum, without recompiling.

## Audit result

A full audit of `teamserver/src/plugins.rs` and `client/src/python.rs` confirms all pyo3 usage is abi3-compatible:
- Types used: `PyModule`, `PyDict`, `PyList`, `PyTuple`, `PyAny`, `Py<T>`, `PyErr` — all in the stable ABI
- `Python::with_gil`, `pyo3::prepare_freethreaded_python`, `PyModule::from_code` — all stable ABI
- `pyo3::ffi::c_str!()` (used in tests) is a pure Rust macro for CStr creation, not a CPython API call — fine in abi3
- No `PyBuffer`, no direct `ffi::*` function calls, no `unsafe` pyo3 blocks

No code changes are needed in the plugin or Python runtime files themselves.

## Changes required

### 1. `teamserver/Cargo.toml` and `client/Cargo.toml`

Change the pyo3 dependency in both crates from:
```toml
pyo3 = "0.23"
```
to:
```toml
pyo3 = { version = "0.23", features = ["abi3-py310"] }
```

`abi3-py310` sets Python 3.10 as the minimum (a reasonable floor for 2026; all major distros ship 3.10+). Alternative: `abi3-py39` if broader compatibility is needed.

### 2. `.cargo/config.toml`

Remove the hardcoded linker search path entirely:
```toml
[build]
rustflags = ["-L", "/usr/lib/python3.12/config-3.12-x86_64-linux-gnu"]
```

With abi3, pyo3 links against `libpython3.so` (the stable ABI shared object) which is always in the standard linker search path. The explicit `-L` flag is no longer needed and actively causes problems on machines without Python 3.12.

### 3. Verify

After the change:
- `cargo build` should succeed without `PYO3_PYTHON` or the `-L` rustflag
- `ldd target/debug/red-cell` should show `libpython3.so.1.0` (not `libpython3.12.so`)
- Run the plugin tests to confirm runtime behaviour is unchanged:
  `cargo test -p red-cell`

## Why abi3-py310

pyo3 docs: https://pyo3.rs/v0.23.0/building-and-distribution#py_limited_api--abi3
The stable ABI guarantees: https://docs.python.org/3/c-api/stable.html
close_reason: Switched client and teamserver PyO3 dependencies to abi3-py310 and removed the pinned Python 3.12 linker search path from .cargo/config.toml.

title: test: operator_agent_info with no parent pivot leaves pivot_parent empty
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/agent_events.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`operator_agent_info` maps `PivotInfo.parent: Option<u32>` to two fields:
- `pivots.parent` — should be `None`
- `pivot_parent` — should be `String::new()` (the empty string fall-through from `unwrap_or_default`)

The existing tests only exercise the `parent: Some(_)` path. The `parent: None` case (a standalone agent that is not a pivot child) is never tested.

If the fall-back logic changed from `unwrap_or_default()` to `unwrap_or("root")` or similar, operators would see a spurious parent ID in the session list and no test would catch it.

**Suggested test approach**:
Call `operator_agent_info` with `PivotInfo { parent: None, children: vec![] }` and assert `info.pivots.parent.is_none()`, `info.pivot_parent.is_empty()`, and `info.pivots.links.is_empty()`.
close_reason: Added test for parent: None path; assert pivot_parent is empty, pivots.parent is None, and pivots.links is empty
